api/BacklashBot.Helpers.SnapshotDiscrepa.title: Class SnapshotDiscrepancyValidator | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Helpers.SnapshotDiscrepa.summary: Class SnapshotDiscrepancyValidator Provides static methods for validating market snapshots to detect data discrepancies and ensure data integrity. This validator is used to filter out invalid snapshots before processing or saving them, preventing downstream issues from corrupted or inconsistent market data. Inheritance object SnapshotDiscrepancyValidator Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: BacklashBot.Helpers Assembly: BacklashBot.dll Syntax public static class SnapshotDiscrepancyValidator Methods | Edit this page View Source ValidateDiscrepancies(MarketSnapshot, double) Validates a market snapshot for various types of discrepancies that could indicate data corruption or invalid market states. This method performs multiple checks including orderbook presence, price overlap detection, and rate consistency validation. Declaration public static SnapshotDiscrepancyValidator.ValidationResult ValidateDiscrepancies(MarketSnapshot snapshot, double threshold = 0.1) Parameters Type Name Description MarketSnapshot snapshot The market snapshot to validate. double threshold The discrepancy threshold for rate validation. Default is 0.1. Returns Type Description SnapshotDiscrepancyValidator.ValidationResult A ValidationResult containing the validation outcome and specific discrepancy flags. Remarks The validation process includes: Checking for missing or empty orderbook data Detecting overlapping bid and ask prices Validating consistency between velocity metrics and actual volumes when change metrics are mature The default 0.1 threshold is considered significant as per user specification. This method is designed to be fast and lightweight, suitable for real-time snapshot processing. Invalid snapshots are logged as warnings in the calling service for monitoring purposes.

api/BacklashBot.Helpers.TradingCalculati.title: Class TradingCalculations | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Helpers.TradingCalculati.summary: Class TradingCalculations Provides static utility methods for various trading calculations, including technical indicators, position metrics, and mathematical operations. This class is used throughout the trading bot for computing EMA, liquidation prices, fees, ROI, and other essential calculations. All methods are designed to be thread-safe and efficient for real-time trading operations. Declaration public static double CalculateBuyinPrice(double marketExposure, int positionSize) Parameters Type Name Description double marketExposure Total market exposure in dollars. Can be negative. int positionSize Number of contracts (positive for Yes, negative for No). Must not be zero. Returns Type Description double Buy-in price per contract, rounded to 2 decimal places. Returns 0 if positionSize is zero. Exceptions Type Condition ArgumentException Thrown if positionSize is zero. | Edit this page View Source CalculateEMA(List<double>, int, double?) Calculates the Exponential Moving Average (EMA) for a list of prices over a specified period. EMA gives more weight to recent prices, making it responsive to price changes. Includes validation for null prices, invalid period, and NaN/Infinity values. Declaration public static double? CalculateEMA(List<double> prices, int period, double? previousEMA = null) Parameters Type Name Description List<double> prices The list of price values. Must not be null, contain at least 'period' elements, and not contain NaN or Infinity. int period The period for the EMA calculation. The calculated EMA value or null if insufficient data, invalid inputs, or invalid result. | Edit this page View Source CalculateEMAAsync(List<double>, int, double?) Asynchronously calculates the Exponential Moving Average (EMA) for a list of prices over a specified period. Useful for high-frequency scenarios where computation can be offloaded. api/BacklashBot.Management.BaseMarketMan.summary: Class BaseMarketManagerService Abstract base class providing common market management functionality for the Kalshi trading bot. Handles market reset operations, interest score calculations, and provides protected methods for adding/removing markets based on various criteria. Serves as the foundation for both managed and unmanaged market management implementations. Now includes configurable queue limits and extracted target calculation service for improved testability. api/BacklashBot.Management.CentralErrorH.title: Class CentralErrorHandler | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Management.CentralErrorH.summary: Class CentralErrorHandler Central error handling service that processes logged errors and warnings from the system, determines catastrophic failure conditions, and triggers appropriate recovery actions. This class acts as the main error coordinator for the Kalshi trading bot, handling various exception types with specific recovery strategies including market resets, connection recovery, and system restarts. Inheritance object CentralErrorHandler Implements ICentralErrorHandler Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: BacklashBot.Management Assembly: BacklashBot.dll Syntax public class CentralErrorHandler : ICentralErrorHandler Remarks The error handler processes errors from a database logging queue and categorizes them as either catastrophic (requiring system restart) or non-catastrophic (handled with market resets or connection recovery). It maintains counters for error tracking and implements threshold-based catastrophic detection based on configurable error frequency parameters. Configuration options include: Error window duration for frequency monitoring Error threshold for catastrophic detection Internet connectivity check parameters (max attempts, delays) Key responsibilities: Process warnings and errors from the logging queue Handle specific exception types with targeted recovery actions Monitor error frequency and detect catastrophic conditions Maintain error statistics and timestamps Trigger market resets for transient failures Manage internet connectivity checks for connection-related errors Validate input parameters to prevent null reference exceptions Constructors | Edit this page View Source CentralErrorHandler(IMarketManagerService, IServiceFactory, DatabaseLoggingQueue, IOptions<ErrorHandlerConfig>, ILogger<ICentralErrorHandler>) Initializes a new instance of the CentralErrorHandler class. Declaration public CentralErrorHandler(IMarketManagerService marketManagerService, IServiceFactory serviceFactory, DatabaseLoggingQueue loggingQueue, IOptions<ErrorHandlerConfig> errorHandlerConfig, ILogger<ICentralErrorHandler> logger) Parameters Type Name Description IMarketManagerService marketManagerService Service for managing market operations and resets. IServiceFactory serviceFactory Factory for creating and accessing various system services. DatabaseLoggingQueue loggingQueue Queue containing logged errors and warnings to be processed. IOptions<ErrorHandlerConfig> errorHandlerConfig Configuration settings for error handling. ILogger<ICentralErrorHandler> logger Logger instance for recording error handler operations. Properties | Edit this page View Source CatastrophicErrorAlreadyDetected Gets or sets a flag indicating whether a catastrophic error has already been detected. Declaration public bool CatastrophicErrorAlreadyDetected { get; set; } Property Value Type Description bool Remarks When set to true, prevents further error processing to avoid redundant handling and focuses on system recovery procedures. | Edit this page View Source ErrorCount Gets or sets the total count of errors processed by the error handler. Declaration public ConcurrentQueue<ErrorHandlerTaskInfo> Errors { get; } Property Value Type Description ConcurrentQueue<ErrorHandlerTaskInfo> | Edit this page View Source LastErrorDate Gets or sets the timestamp of the last error occurrence. Declaration public DateTime LastErrorDate { get; set; } Property Value Type Description DateTime | Edit this page View Source LastSuccessfulSnapshot Gets or sets the timestamp of the last successful snapshot creation. Declaration public DateTime LastSuccessfulSnapshot { get; set; } Property Value Type Description DateTime Remarks Used to detect prolonged periods without successful snapshots, which may indicate a catastrophic system failure. | Edit this page View Source NonCatastrophicErrorCount Gets the current count of non-catastrophic errors within the monitoring window. Declaration public int NonCatastrophicErrorCount { get; } Property Value Type Description int Remarks This count is used to determine if the error threshold has been exceeded, which would trigger a catastrophic failure condition. | Edit this page View Source WarningCount Gets or sets the total count of warnings processed by the error handler. Declaration public long WarningCount { get; set; } Property Value Type Description long | Edit this page View Source Warnings Gets the queue of pending warnings to be processed. Adds an error to the processing queue for immediate handling. If null, a default exception is created. string identifier Identifier for the source of the error (e.g., service or component name). string message Optional custom message to use instead of the exception message. Remarks Errors are processed during the next HandleErrors() call. This method increments the error count and updates the last error timestamp. | Edit this page View Source AddWarning(Exception, string, string?) Adds a warning to the processing queue for later handling. Declaration public void AddWarning(Exception ex, string identifier, string? message = null) Parameters Type Name Description Exception ex The exception associated with the warning, if any. string identifier Identifier for the source of the warning (e.g., service or component name). string message Optional custom message to use instead of the exception message. Remarks Warnings are processed during the next HandleErrors() call. If an exception is provided, it may be converted to an error if it matches certain criteria during processing. | Edit this page View Source CheckInternetConnection() Performs a series of internet connectivity checks with exponential backoff retry logic. Declaration public Task<bool> CheckInternetConnection() Returns Type Description Task<bool> True if internet connection is confirmed; false if all attempts fail. Remarks This method attempts to verify internet connectivity with configurable parameters. Used primarily during system startup to ensure network availability before proceeding with dashboard initialization. | Edit this page View Source HandleErrors() Processes all pending errors and warnings from the logging queue, determines if any constitute catastrophic failures, and triggers appropriate recovery actions. Declaration public Task<bool> HandleErrors() Returns Type Description Task<bool> True if a catastrophic error was detected requiring system restart; otherwise false. Remarks This method performs the following operations: Dequeues all errors and warnings from the logging queue Processes warnings, converting those with exceptions to errors if necessary Handles each error based on its exception type with specific recovery strategies Monitors error frequency and triggers catastrophic failure if thresholds are exceeded Cleans up old non-catastrophic errors and clears processed queues Specific exception handling includes: MarketInterestScoreDeadlockException: Logged as handled ConnectionDisruptionException: Attempts WebSocket reconnection KnownDuplicateInsertException: Logged as handled Various market-related exceptions: Triggers market resets WebSocketRetryFailedException: Marked as catastrophic Internet connectivity issues: Marked as catastrophic Overseer connection failures: Logged as informational (not catastrophic) Unhandled exceptions: Marked as catastrophic Implements ICentralErrorHandler

api/BacklashBot.Management.CentralPerfor.summary: Class CentralPerformanceMonitor Central performance monitoring service that tracks system performance metrics, records execution times, and monitors queue depths for the Kalshi trading bot. This class provides comprehensive performance analytics including WebSocket event rates, API execution times, and queue utilization metrics with configurable alerting. api/BacklashBot.Management.html.title: Namespace BacklashBot.Management | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Management.html.summary: Namespace BacklashBot.Management Classes BaseMarketManagerService Abstract base class providing common market management functionality for the Kalshi trading bot. Handles market reset operations, interest score calculations, and provides protected methods for adding/removing markets based on various criteria. Serves as the foundation for both managed and unmanaged market management implementations. Now includes configurable queue limits and extracted target calculation service for improved testability. BrainStatusService Service responsible for managing the initialization and status of a brain instance, providing access to the brain lock and session identifier in a thread-safe manner. CentralBrain Central orchestrator for the Kalshi trading bot system. Manages the complete bot lifecycle including startup, shutdown, market monitoring, snapshot creation, and error handling. Acts as the main coordination point between various services and components. CentralErrorHandler Central error handling service that processes logged errors and warnings from the system, determines catastrophic failure conditions, and triggers appropriate recovery actions. This class acts as the main error coordinator for the Kalshi trading bot, handling various exception types with specific recovery strategies including market resets, connection recovery, and system restarts. CentralPerformanceMonitor Central performance monitoring service that tracks system performance metrics, records execution times, and monitors queue depths for the Kalshi trading bot. This class provides comprehensive performance analytics including WebSocket event rates, API execution times, and queue utilization metrics with configurable alerting. ManagedMarketManagerService Implements managed market management strategy for the Kalshi trading bot. Uses dynamic target calculation based on performance metrics rather than fixed counts. Automatically adjusts market watch counts based on system usage, queue depths, and performance indicators to optimize resource utilization and market coverage. Includes input validation for brain configuration parameters with warning logs for invalid values. MarketAnalysisHelper Helper service for performing market analysis operations, specifically generating snapshot groups from raw market snapshots by filtering markets and processing them into valid time periods. MarketManagerService Main market management service that coordinates between managed and unmanaged market management strategies. Acts as a facade that delegates watch list monitoring to the appropriate implementation based on the brain instance's ManagedWatchList configuration. Provides a unified interface for market management operations while allowing different strategies for different brain configurations. Now includes configurable queue limits and extracted target calculation service for improved testability. OvernightActivitiesHelper TargetCalculationService Service for calculating optimal target number of markets to watch based on performance metrics. Implements configurable queue limits and provides detailed logging of target calculations. UnmanagedMarketManagerService Implements unmanaged market management strategy for the Kalshi trading bot. Uses fixed target watch counts rather than dynamic calculation based on performance metrics. Focuses on maintaining a stable number of markets while removing ended markets and replacing uninteresting markets with higher-interest alternatives. Includes input validation for brain configuration parameters with warning logs for invalid values.

api/BacklashBot.Middleware.SignalRAuthen.title: Class SignalRAuthenticationMiddleware | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Middleware.SignalRAuthen.summary: Class SignalRAuthenticationMiddleware Middleware for authenticating SignalR clients in the BacklashBot trading system. This middleware intercepts SignalR method invocations and connection events to validate client credentials, ensuring only authenticated clients can access sensitive hub methods. It integrates with the database to verify client tokens and manage connection state, providing a secure layer for real-time communication between trading clients and the server. Inheritance object SignalRAuthenticationMiddleware Implements IHubFilter Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: BacklashBot.Middleware Assembly: BacklashBot.dll Syntax public class SignalRAuthenticationMiddleware : IHubFilter Constructors | Edit this page View Source SignalRAuthenticationMiddleware(IServiceFactory, ILogger<SignalRAuthenticationMiddleware>) Initializes a new instance of the SignalRAuthenticationMiddleware class. Declaration public SignalRAuthenticationMiddleware(IServiceFactory serviceFactory, ILogger<SignalRAuthenticationMiddleware> logger) Parameters Type Name Description IServiceFactory serviceFactory Factory for creating service instances, used to access database context and other services. ILogger<SignalRAuthenticationMiddleware> logger Logger instance for recording authentication events and errors. Methods | Edit this page View Source InvokeMethodAsync(HubInvocationContext, Func<HubInvocationContext, ValueTask<object?>>) Intercepts SignalR method invocations to enforce authentication for protected hub methods. Checks if the invoked method requires authentication and validates the client's credentials before allowing the method to proceed. If authentication fails, throws an exception to prevent unauthorized access. Declaration public ValueTask<object?> InvokeMethodAsync(HubInvocationContext invocationContext, Func<HubInvocationContext, ValueTask<object?>> next) Parameters Type Name Description HubInvocationContext invocationContext Context containing information about the hub method being invoked. Func<HubInvocationContext, ValueTask<object>> next Delegate to the next middleware or hub method in the pipeline. Returns Type Description ValueTask<object> The result of the hub method invocation. | Edit this page View Source OnConnectedAsync(HubLifetimeContext, Func<HubLifetimeContext, Task>) Handles client connection events in the SignalR pipeline. Attempts to authenticate the client using query parameters and updates the database with the connection information. If authentication fails, the connection is aborted. If no authentication parameters are provided, the connection is allowed but marked as unauthenticated. Declaration public Task OnConnectedAsync(HubLifetimeContext context, Func<HubLifetimeContext, Task> next) Parameters Type Name Description HubLifetimeContext context Context containing information about the connection event. Func<HubLifetimeContext, Task> next Delegate to the next middleware or hub in the pipeline. Returns Type Description Task A task representing the asynchronous operation. | Edit this page View Source OnDisconnectedAsync(HubLifetimeContext, Exception?, Func<HubLifetimeContext, Exception?, Task>) Handles client disconnection events in the SignalR pipeline. Logs the disconnection for monitoring purposes and allows the pipeline to continue. api/BacklashBot.Middleware.html.title: Namespace BacklashBot.Middleware | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Middleware.html.summary: Namespace BacklashBot.Middleware Classes SignalRAuthenticationMiddleware Middleware for authenticating SignalR clients in the BacklashBot trading system. This middleware intercepts SignalR method invocations and connection events to validate client credentials, ensuring only authenticated clients can access sensitive hub methods. It integrates with the database to verify client tokens and manage connection state, providing a secure layer for real-time communication between trading clients and the server.

api/BacklashBot.Services.InterestScoreSe.title: Class InterestScoreService | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Services.InterestScoreSe.summary: Class InterestScoreService Service responsible for calculating interest scores for Kalshi markets based on various trading metrics. This service evaluates market attractiveness by analyzing spread characteristics, trading volume, liquidity patterns, and market continuity to provide quantitative scores for market selection. Features: Configurable cache duration for performance optimization Comprehensive performance metrics collection and monitoring Input validation for market tickers and weight parameters Thread-safe operations for concurrent access Automatic metrics logging with configurable intervals Inheritance object InterestScoreService Implements IInterestScoreService Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: BacklashBot.Services Assembly: BacklashBot.dll Syntax public class InterestScoreService : IInterestScoreService Constructors | Edit this page View Source InterestScoreService(ILogger<IInterestScoreService>, IOptions<InterestScoreConfig>) Initializes a new instance of the InterestScoreService class. IOptions<InterestScoreConfig> config The configuration options for the interest score service, including cache duration and performance metrics settings. Methods | Edit this page View Source CalculateMarketInterestScoreAsync(IKalshiBotContext, string, double, double, double, double, double, double, double) Calculates a comprehensive interest score for a specific market ticker. The score is computed using multiple weighted factors including spread characteristics, trading volume, liquidity, open interest, and market continuity. Thresholds and maximum values are cached for performance and recalculated periodically based on configurable cache duration settings. Features: Input validation for ticker and weight parameters Configurable cache duration for performance optimization Performance metrics collection for operation timing Cache hit/miss tracking for monitoring efficiency Comprehensive error handling with detailed logging Declaration public Task<(double score, (double spreadTightness, double spreadWidth, double volume, double volumePercentile, double liquidityPercentile, double openInterestPercentile, double continuity) scoreParts)> CalculateMarketInterestScoreAsync(IKalshiBotContext dbContext, string marketTicker, double spreadTightnessWeight = 0.2, double spreadWidthWeight = 0.15, double volumeWeight = 0.2, double volumePercentileWeight = 0.1, double liquidityPercentileWeight = 0.09, double openInterestPercentileWeight = 0.065, double continuityWeight = 0.145) Parameters Type Name Description IKalshiBotContext dbContext Database context for accessing market data. string marketTicker The market ticker symbol to score. Must be within valid range. double volumeWeight Weight for volume factor (0.0-1.0). Must be within valid range. double volumePercentileWeight Weight for volume percentile factor (0.0-1.0). api/BacklashBot.Services.MarketDataIniti.title: Class MarketDataInitializer | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Services.MarketDataIniti.summary: Class MarketDataInitializer Service responsible for initializing market data during application startup. This service fetches watched markets, subscribes to WebSocket channels for new markets, synchronizes market data, and sets up positions and account balance. It ensures that all necessary market data is available before the application becomes fully operational. Inheritance object MarketDataInitializer Implements IMarketDataInitializer Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: BacklashBot.Services Assembly: BacklashBot.dll Syntax public class MarketDataInitializer : IMarketDataInitializer Constructors | Edit this page View Source MarketDataInitializer(ILogger<IMarketDataInitializer>, IServiceFactory, IScopeManagerService, IBotReadyStatus, IStatusTrackerService) Initializes a new instance of the MarketDataInitializer class. Declaration public MarketDataInitializer(ILogger<IMarketDataInitializer> logger, IServiceFactory serviceFactory, IScopeManagerService scopeManagerService, IBotReadyStatus readyStatus, IStatusTrackerService statusTracker) Parameters Type Name Description ILogger<IMarketDataInitializer> logger Logger for recording initialization operations and errors. IServiceFactory serviceFactory Factory for accessing other services in the application. IScopeManagerService scopeManagerService Service for managing dependency injection scopes. IBotReadyStatus readyStatus Service tracking the application's readiness status. IStatusTrackerService statusTracker Service for tracking application status and cancellation tokens. Properties | Edit this page View Source LastInitializationDuration Gets the duration of the last market data initialization operation. Declaration public TimeSpan LastInitializationDuration { get; } Property Value Type Description TimeSpan | Edit this page View Source LastInitializationMarketCount Gets the number of markets processed during the last initialization. Declaration public int LastInitializationMarketCount { get; } Property Value Type Description int Methods | Edit this page View Source SetupAsync() Performs the complete market data initialization sequence. This method fetches watched markets, subscribes to WebSocket channels, synchronizes market data, retrieves positions, and updates account balance. It runs operations sequentially on a low-priority thread to avoid interfering with other system processes. api/BacklashBot.Services.MarketRefreshSe.summary: Class MarketRefreshService Service responsible for managing periodic market data refresh operations for watched markets. This service runs a background task that checks trading status, determines which markets need data synchronization, and performs refresh operations at configured intervals. It provides metrics on refresh performance and supports immediate refresh triggers for specific markets. api/BacklashBot.Services.OrderbookChange.title: Class OrderbookChangeTracker | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Services.OrderbookChange.summary: Class OrderbookChangeTracker Tracks and analyzes orderbook changes for a specific market in the Kalshi trading system. This class is responsible for processing orderbook snapshots, recording individual changes, matching trades to orderbook changes, calculating various market metrics (velocity, volume, rates), and maintaining a rolling window of orderbook events for analysis. It implements the IOrderbookChangeTracker interface and integrates with the broader trading bot ecosystem to provide real-time market data analysis and metrics calculation. Inheritance object OrderbookChangeTracker Implements IOrderbookChangeTracker IDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: BacklashBot.Services Assembly: BacklashBot.dll Syntax public class OrderbookChangeTracker : IOrderbookChangeTracker, IDisposable Remarks The tracker maintains concurrent queues for orderbook changes and trade events, using timers for periodic metric recalculation and cleanup of old events. It employs locking mechanisms to ensure thread safety during matching operations and supports cancellation tokens for graceful shutdown. Metrics are calculated over configurable time windows and include velocity per minute, trade rates, average trade sizes, and order volume analysis. ProcessOrderbookSnapshot(List<OrderbookData>, List<OrderbookData>) Processes a complete orderbook snapshot by comparing it with the previous state. Calculates deltas for each price level, records individual orderbook changes, and triggers metric recalculation. Handles market reset scenarios when snapshots are empty or when significant time gaps are detected. Declaration public void ProcessOrderbookSnapshot(List<OrderbookData> originalOrderbook, List<OrderbookData> newOrderbook) Parameters Type Name Description List<OrderbookData> originalOrderbook The previous orderbook state, or null for initial snapshot List<OrderbookData> newOrderbook The current orderbook state to process Remarks This method is called periodically with full orderbook data. It compares the new snapshot against the previous one to identify changes, then records each change individually. If the original orderbook is null or a 2-minute gap is detected, it resets the event queues and recalculates metrics to ensure data consistency. | Edit this page View Source RecalculateAllMetrics() Triggers a complete recalculation of all market metrics including cleanup of old events. This method performs standard metrics recalculation and updates current snapshot metrics. api/BacklashBot.Services.OverseerClientS.title: Class OverseerClientService | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Services.OverseerClientS.summary: Class OverseerClientService Manages the client-side connection to an Overseer server for monitoring and oversight of the trading bot. This service establishes and maintains a SignalR connection to an Overseer instance, handles periodic check-ins with system status information, and automatically discovers and switches to better overseer servers when available. It provides resilience through automatic reconnection, connection validation, and failover mechanisms. The service integrates with the broader bot ecosystem to report market data, error counts, and performance metrics. Inheritance object OverseerClientService Implements IOverseerClientService Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: BacklashBot.Services Assembly: BacklashBot.dll Syntax public class OverseerClientService : IOverseerClientService Remarks The OverseerClientService is designed to be optional - if no overseer is available, the bot continues operating without oversight. It discovers available overseer instances from the database only, prioritizing production instances over those named "DevInstance", and selects the most appropriate one based on recency of heartbeat. api/BacklashBot.Services.ServiceFactory..title: Class ServiceFactory | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Services.ServiceFactory..summary: Class ServiceFactory Provides centralized access to various services within the Kalshi trading bot system through dependency injection scopes. This factory implements the service locator pattern to manage service initialization, configuration validation, and thread-safe access to scoped services. It acts as a bridge between the application's dependency injection container and service consumers, ensuring proper scope management and resource cleanup. Services are cached per scope to improve performance by avoiding repeated dependency injection resolution. api/BacklashBot.Services.TradingCalculat.title: Class TradingCalculator | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Services.TradingCalculat.summary: Class TradingCalculator Provides calculations for various technical indicators used in trading strategies for the Kalshi marketplace. This class implements the ITradingCalculator interface and computes indicators such as RSI, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic Oscillator, ATR, VWAP, OBV, support/resistance levels, PSAR, and ADX based on pseudo-candlestick data. All calculations are designed for event-driven contracts with prices bounded between 1 and 99 cents. Depends on CalculationConfig for indicator parameters, includes async methods for performance in high-frequency scenarios, and incorporates comprehensive input validation including null checks, empty list handling, and data integrity checks with warnings. Inheritance object TradingCalculator Implements ITradingCalculator Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: BacklashBot.Services Assembly: BacklashBot.dll Syntax public class TradingCalculator : ITradingCalculator Remarks Indicators are calculated using standard formulas adapted for Kalshi's binary outcome markets. Null values are returned when insufficient data is available for reliable calculations. Input validation ensures data quality by checking for null lists, empty collections, and invalid candlestick data (e.g., negative prices, invalid timestamps). Constructors | Edit this page View Source TradingCalculator(ILogger<ITradingCalculator>, CalculationConfig) Initializes a new instance of the TradingCalculator class with the specified logger and configuration. The CalculationConfig provides parameters for indicator calculations such as PSAR acceleration factors, ADX periods, and exponential multipliers. Declaration public TradingCalculator(ILogger<ITradingCalculator> logger, CalculationConfig config) Parameters Type Name Description ILogger<ITradingCalculator> logger The logger instance for recording calculation events and errors. CalculationConfig config The CalculationConfig instance containing parameters for technical indicator calculations. Exceptions Type Condition ArgumentNullException Thrown when logger or config is null. Methods | Edit this page View Source CalculateADX(List<PseudoCandlestick>) Calculates the Average Directional Index (ADX) value along with +DI and -DI for a series of pseudo-candlesticks in the Kalshi marketplace. This indicator serves as a robust complementary metric, particularly when integrated with tools like the Parabolic Stop and Reverse (PSAR) to validate trend strength. It provides an objective measure of trend intensity, irrespective of direction, which aids in distinguishing genuine trends from periods of consolidation or volatility without momentum. This is especially pertinent in Kalshi's event-driven environment, where contract prices may exhibit pronounced trends due to evolving probabilities influenced by economic announcements or other developments. By confirming the presence of a strong trend (typically ADX values exceeding 25), it enhances the efficacy of PSAR signals, reducing the likelihood of whipsaws in non-trending conditions. The +DI and -DI components provide directional information: +DI > -DI suggests uptrend, -DI > +DI suggests downtrend. Its utility diminishes in highly volatile, range-bound markets common to certain Kalshi contracts awaiting resolution. Declaration public (double? ADX, double? PlusDI, double? MinusDI) CalculateADX(List<PseudoCandlestick> pseudoCandles) Parameters Type Name Description List<PseudoCandlestick> pseudoCandles The list of pseudo-candlesticks containing mid-high, mid-low, and mid-close prices. Returns Type Description (double? MACD, double? Signal, double? Histogram) A tuple containing ADX, +DI, and -DI values, or null values if insufficient data or invalid computation. Remarks Input validation includes null check for the pseudo-candlesticks list, empty list handling with warnings, and data integrity checks with warnings for invalid candlestick data (e.g., negative prices, invalid timestamps). Parameters are sourced from configuration where applicable (e.g., ADX period). Interpretations of ADX values are as follows in Kalshi's context: Below 20: Indicates a weak or absent trend, suggesting consolidation or choppy conditions; traders should exercise caution with trend-following strategies like PSAR, as signals may prove unreliable. Between 20 and 25: Signals the potential emergence of a trend; this transitional range warrants monitoring for confirmation, such as a PSAR crossover aligned with rising ADX. Between 25 and 40: Reflects a strong trend, validating PSAR positions and encouraging trend continuation trades, with higher values denoting increased momentum. Above 40: Denotes an exceptionally strong trend, often nearing potential exhaustion; while supportive of PSAR trailing stops, it may foreshadow reversals, prompting consideration of profit-taking. +DI and -DI interpretations: +DI > -DI: Suggests upward trend strength -DI > +DI: Suggests downward trend strength +DI crossing above -DI: Potential bullish signal -DI crossing above +DI: Potential bearish signal CalculateADXAsync(List<PseudoCandlestick>) | Edit this page View Source CalculateADXAsync(List<PseudoCandlestick>) Calculates the Average Directional Index (ADX) value asynchronously along with +DI and -DI for a series of pseudo-candlesticks in the Kalshi marketplace. This indicator serves as a robust complementary metric, particularly when integrated with tools like the Parabolic Stop and Reverse (PSAR) to validate trend strength. It provides an objective measure of trend intensity, irrespective of direction, which aids in distinguishing genuine trends from periods of consolidation or volatility without momentum. This is especially pertinent in Kalshi's event-driven environment, where contract prices may exhibit pronounced trends due to evolving probabilities influenced by economic announcements or other developments. By confirming the presence of a strong trend (typically ADX values exceeding 25), it enhances the efficacy of PSAR signals, reducing the likelihood of whipsaws in non-trending conditions. The +DI and -DI components provide directional information: +DI > -DI suggests uptrend, -DI > +DI suggests downtrend. Its utility diminishes in highly volatile, range-bound markets common to certain Kalshi contracts awaiting resolution. Declaration public Task<(double? ADX, double? PlusDI, double? MinusDI)> CalculateADXAsync(List<PseudoCandlestick> pseudoCandles) Parameters Type Name Description List<PseudoCandlestick> pseudoCandles The list of pseudo-candlesticks containing mid-high, mid-low, and mid-close prices. Histogram)> A task that represents the asynchronous operation, containing a tuple of ADX, +DI, and -DI values, or null values if insufficient data or invalid computation. True range is the maximum of: current high - current low, |current high - previous close|, |current low - previous close|. Higher ATR values indicate higher volatility. Declaration public double? CalculateBollingerBands(List<PseudoCandlestick>, int, double) | Edit this page View Source CalculateHistoricalSupportResistance(string, List<CandlestickData>, double, int, double, int) Calculates statistically significant historical support and resistance levels for a given market ticker. This method identifies key price levels by smoothing the frequency of price occurrences using a Gaussian filter, detecting peaks in the smoothed distribution, and ensuring a minimum distance between levels to avoid clustering. The strength of each level is calculated by combining the smoothed frequency with the total trading volume around the peak price (±1 cent). It uses the average of AskClose and BidClose prices for normalization. Price levels must have a minimum number of touches within ±1 cent of the peak, defined as a percentage of total valid candlesticks. Candlesticks with UTC timestamps between 07:00:00 and 11:59:00 (truncated to minutes) are excluded from the analysis. Returns Type Description List<SupportResistanceLevel> A list of SupportResistanceLevel objects, sorted by strength in descending order. CalculateOBV(List<PseudoCandlestick>) | Edit this page View Source CalculatePSAR(List<PseudoCandlestick>) Calculates the Parabolic Stop and Reverse (PSAR) value for a series of pseudo-candlesticks in the Kalshi marketplace. This indicator is valuable for event-driven contracts where price trends can emerge rapidly due to evolving news or data releases influencing outcome probabilities. It identifies trend directions and provides dynamic trailing stop levels, aiding risk management in volatile, bounded-price environments (1 to 99 cents per contract). However, in ranging or sideways markets common when events are distant or uncertain PSAR may generate frequent false reversal signals (whipsaws), potentially leading to unnecessary trades. It performs best when combined with trend-confirming indicators like the Average Directional Index (ADX) to filter signals. Declaration public double? CalculatePSAR(List<PseudoCandlestick> pseudoCandles) Parameters Type Name Description List<PseudoCandlestick> pseudoCandles Returns Type Description double? The current PSAR value, or null if insufficient data or invalid computation. Remarks Input validation includes null check for the candlesticks list and parameter validation with exceptions. Parameters are sourced from configuration where applicable (e.g., PSAR acceleration factors). Different PSAR values indicate the following in Kalshi's context: A PSAR value below the current price (e.g., PSAR at 45 when the mid-close is 60) suggests an uptrend, signaling potential buying opportunities or holding long ("Yes") positions, with the PSAR serving as a rising trailing stop. A PSAR value above the current price (e.g., PSAR at 75 when the mid-close is 60) indicates a downtrend, implying selling pressure or short ("No") positions, with the PSAR acting as a falling trailing stop. Crossovers, where the price moves beyond the PSAR, denote potential trend reversals: a price rising above PSAR flips to bullish, while falling below flips to bearish. Acceleration factor (AF) increases (up to the maximum, typically 0.20) amplify the PSAR's sensitivity in strong trends, tightening stops to lock in gains. api/BacklashBot.Services.TradingSnapshot.title: Class TradingSnapshotService | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Services.TradingSnapshot.summary: Class TradingSnapshotService Service responsible for managing trading snapshot operations, including saving market data snapshots to disk and loading them for analysis. This service handles snapshot validation, timing controls, and schema management to ensure reliable data persistence and retrieval for trading strategy evaluation. Inheritance object TradingSnapshotService Implements ITradingSnapshotService Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: BacklashBot.Services Assembly: BacklashBot.dll Syntax public class TradingSnapshotService : ITradingSnapshotService Remarks The service implements timing-based snapshot saving with tolerance windows to handle irregular market data arrival. It uses parallel processing for efficient loading of multiple snapshots and maintains schema compatibility through version-based JSON sanitization. Snapshots are stored as JSON files in a configured directory for later analysis by trading strategies and backtesting systems. IOptions<TradingConfig> tradingConfig Configuration options for trading parameters including decision frequency. IServiceScopeFactory scopeFactory Factory for creating service scopes to access database services. Declaration public DateTime? NextExpectedSnapshotTimestamp { get; set; } Property Value Type Description DateTime? Remarks This property is used to detect timing irregularities in snapshot arrival. It is updated after each successful snapshot save and reset when snapshot tracking is cleared. Null indicates no expected timing has been established. Failed deserializations are logged as warnings but don't stop processing of other snapshots. api/BacklashBot.Services.WebSocketMonito.summary: Class WebSocketMonitorService Service responsible for monitoring the Kalshi exchange status and managing WebSocket connection lifecycle. This service periodically checks the exchange's operational status and automatically connects/disconnects the WebSocket client based on exchange availability and bot initialization state. It ensures reliable real-time data streaming while handling connection failures and exchange downtime gracefully. api/BacklashBot.Services.html.title: Namespace BacklashBot.Services | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Services.html.summary: Namespace BacklashBot.Services Classes BroadcastService Implements the broadcast service for real-time system status communication. Handles periodic broadcasting of comprehensive system health data to connected clients including performance metrics, market information, and operational status. CandlestickService Service responsible for managing candlestick data for trading markets. Handles loading, processing, and persistence of historical price data from both Parquet files and SQL database, including forward filling and deduplication. Provides market data population and candlestick synchronization functionality. HealthCheckService Implementation of IHealthCheckService to verify dependent services are operational. InterestScoreService Service responsible for calculating interest scores for Kalshi markets based on various trading metrics. This service evaluates market attractiveness by analyzing spread characteristics, trading volume, liquidity patterns, and market continuity to provide quantitative scores for market selection. Features: Configurable cache duration for performance optimization Comprehensive performance metrics collection and monitoring Input validation for market tickers and weight parameters Thread-safe operations for concurrent access Automatic metrics logging with configurable intervals KaslhiBotScopeManagerService Service for managing dependency injection scopes in the Kalshi trading bot system. This service handles the creation, initialization, and disposal of service scopes, ensuring proper resource management and access to scoped services throughout the application. MarketDataInitializer Service responsible for initializing market data during application startup. This service fetches watched markets, subscribes to WebSocket channels for new markets, synchronizes market data, and sets up positions and account balance. It ensures that all necessary market data is available before the application becomes fully operational. MarketDataService Core service responsible for managing market data operations, WebSocket event handling, market watchlist management, and real-time data synchronization for the Kalshi trading bot. Supports configurable timeouts and batch sizes via MarketDataConfig, data validation for WebSocket messages, and retry logic with Polly for resilient API calls. MarketRefreshService Service responsible for managing periodic market data refresh operations for watched markets. This service runs a background task that checks trading status, determines which markets need data synchronization, and performs refresh operations at configured intervals. It provides metrics on refresh performance and supports immediate refresh triggers for specific markets. OrderBookService Service responsible for managing order book data for Kalshi markets. Handles WebSocket order book events, processes snapshots and deltas asynchronously, maintains thread-safe access to order book data, and provides synchronization capabilities. Integrates with the broader trading bot ecosystem to ensure real-time order book updates. OrderBookServiceConfig OrderbookChangeTracker Tracks and analyzes orderbook changes for a specific market in the Kalshi trading system. This class is responsible for processing orderbook snapshots, recording individual changes, matching trades to orderbook changes, calculating various market metrics (velocity, volume, rates), and maintaining a rolling window of orderbook events for analysis. It implements the IOrderbookChangeTracker interface and integrates with the broader trading bot ecosystem to provide real-time market data analysis and metrics calculation. OverseerClientService Manages the client-side connection to an Overseer server for monitoring and oversight of the trading bot. This service establishes and maintains a SignalR connection to an Overseer instance, handles periodic check-ins with system status information, and automatically discovers and switches to better overseer servers when available. It provides resilience through automatic reconnection, connection validation, and failover mechanisms. The service integrates with the broader bot ecosystem to report market data, error counts, and performance metrics. ServiceFactory Provides centralized access to various services within the Kalshi trading bot system through dependency injection scopes. This factory implements the service locator pattern to manage service initialization, configuration validation, and thread-safe access to scoped services. It acts as a bridge between the application's dependency injection container and service consumers, ensuring proper scope management and resource cleanup. Services are cached per scope to improve performance by avoiding repeated dependency injection resolution. TradingCalculator Provides calculations for various technical indicators used in trading strategies for the Kalshi marketplace. This class implements the ITradingCalculator interface and computes indicators such as RSI, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic Oscillator, ATR, VWAP, OBV, support/resistance levels, PSAR, and ADX based on pseudo-candlestick data. All calculations are designed for event-driven contracts with prices bounded between 1 and 99 cents. Depends on CalculationConfig for indicator parameters, includes async methods for performance in high-frequency scenarios, and incorporates comprehensive input validation including null checks, empty list handling, and data integrity checks with warnings. TradingSnapshotService Service responsible for managing trading snapshot operations, including saving market data snapshots to disk and loading them for analysis. This service handles snapshot validation, timing controls, and schema management to ensure reliable data persistence and retrieval for trading strategy evaluation. WebSocketMonitorService Service responsible for monitoring the Kalshi exchange status and managing WebSocket connection lifecycle. This service periodically checks the exchange's operational status and automatically connects/disconnects the WebSocket client based on exchange availability and bot initialization state. It ensures reliable real-time data streaming while handling connection failures and exchange downtime gracefully.

api/BacklashBot.State.KalshiBotReadyStat.title: Class KalshiBotReadyStatus | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.State.KalshiBotReadyStat.summary: Class KalshiBotReadyStatus Manages the readiness status of different components within the Kalshi trading bot system. This class provides TaskCompletionSource objects to signal when various parts of the bot have completed their initialization or are ready for operation. Inheritance object KalshiBotReadyStatus Implements IBotReadyStatus Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: BacklashBot.State Assembly: BacklashBot.dll Syntax public class KalshiBotReadyStatus : IBotReadyStatus Remarks The readiness status system allows different components to signal their completion state: InitializationCompleted: Signals when the bot's core initialization is finished BrowserReady: Signals when browser-related components are ready (if applicable) Components can await these TaskCompletionSource objects to coordinate startup sequences, and can set the results to signal completion to waiting components. This class includes logging for state changes, input validation, and metrics collection for better debugging and monitoring. Constructors | Edit this page View Source KalshiBotReadyStatus(ILogger<KalshiBotReadyStatus>) Initializes a new instance of the KalshiBotReadyStatus class. Creates the initial TaskCompletionSource objects and sets up the default state. Declaration public KalshiBotReadyStatus(ILogger<KalshiBotReadyStatus> logger) Parameters Type Name Description ILogger<KalshiBotReadyStatus> logger The logger for tracking state changes and operations. Properties | Edit this page View Source BrowserReady Gets or sets the TaskCompletionSource that signals when browser-related components are ready. This is used for components that depend on browser initialization or UI readiness. Declaration public TaskCompletionSource<bool> BrowserReady { get; set; } Property Value Type Description TaskCompletionSource<bool> The TaskCompletionSource for browser readiness signaling. | Edit this page View Source InitializationCompleted Gets or sets the TaskCompletionSource that signals when the bot's core initialization is complete. Components can await this task to wait for initialization to finish, or set its result to signal completion. Declaration public TaskCompletionSource<bool> InitializationCompleted { get; set; } Property Value Type Description TaskCompletionSource<bool> The TaskCompletionSource for initialization completion signaling. Methods | Edit this page View Source ResetAll() Resets all readiness status indicators to their initial state. This creates new TaskCompletionSource objects and sets the initialization status to false. Declaration public void ResetAll() Remarks This method is typically called when restarting the bot or when a reset is needed to clear all previous readiness signals and start fresh. | Edit this page View Source SetBrowserReady(bool) Sets the browser ready status with validation and logging. Declaration public void SetBrowserReady(bool result) Parameters Type Name Description bool result The result to set for browser readiness. Exceptions Type Condition ArgumentNullException Thrown when TaskCompletionSource is null. InvalidOperationException Thrown when trying to set result on already completed task. | Edit this page View Source SetInitializationCompleted(bool) Sets the initialization completion status with validation and logging. Declaration public void SetInitializationCompleted(bool result) Parameters Type Name Description bool result The result to set for initialization completion. Exceptions Type Condition ArgumentNullException Thrown when TaskCompletionSource is null. InvalidOperationException Thrown when trying to set result on already completed task. Implements IBotReadyStatus

api/BacklashBot.State.KalshiBotStatusTra.title: Class KalshiBotStatusTracker | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.State.KalshiBotStatusTra.summary: Class KalshiBotStatusTracker Manages the global cancellation state for the Kalshi trading bot system. This class provides a centralized CancellationTokenSource that can be used to coordinate graceful shutdown and cancellation across all components of the bot. Inheritance object KalshiBotStatusTracker Implements IStatusTrackerService IDisposable IAsyncDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: BacklashBot.State Assembly: BacklashBot.dll Syntax public class KalshiBotStatusTracker : IStatusTrackerService, IDisposable, IAsyncDisposable Remarks The status tracker ensures thread-safe access to the global cancellation token and provides: A single CancellationToken that all components can monitor for shutdown signals Thread-safe cancellation operations Proper resource cleanup through IDisposable and IAsyncDisposable implementation Reset capability for restarting the cancellation state Logging for cancellation operations and metrics collection This is registered as a singleton service to ensure all components share the same cancellation state. Constructors | Edit this page View Source KalshiBotStatusTracker(ILogger<KalshiBotStatusTracker>) Initializes a new instance of the KalshiBotStatusTracker class. Creates the initial CancellationTokenSource and sets up the cancellation state. Methods | Edit this page View Source CancelAll() Cancels all operations by triggering the global cancellation token. This signals all components that are monitoring the cancellation token to stop their operations. Declaration public void CancelAll() Remarks This method is thread-safe and should be called when the bot needs to shut down. After calling this method, the cancellation token will be in a cancelled state. | Edit this page View Source Dispose() Disposes of the resources used by the status tracker. This cancels any pending operations and disposes of the CancellationTokenSource. Declaration public void Dispose() Remarks This method should be called when the status tracker is no longer needed, typically during application shutdown. It ensures proper cleanup of unmanaged resources. | Edit this page View Source DisposeAsync() Asynchronously disposes of the resources used by the status tracker. This provides an async disposal pattern for better resource management in async contexts. Remarks This method provides thread-safe access to the cancellation token. Components should regularly check this token or use it in async operations to respond to shutdown requests gracefully. | Edit this page View Source GetMetrics() Gets metrics information about the status tracker. This clears any previous cancellation state and allows the bot to restart operations. Declaration public void ResetAll() Remarks This method properly disposes of the old CancellationTokenSource before creating a new one. api/BacklashBot.State.html.title: Namespace BacklashBot.State | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.State.html.summary: Namespace BacklashBot.State Classes DataCache KalshiBotReadyStatus Manages the readiness status of different components within the Kalshi trading bot system. This class provides TaskCompletionSource objects to signal when various parts of the bot have completed their initialization or are ready for operation. KalshiBotStatusTracker Manages the global cancellation state for the Kalshi trading bot system. This class provides a centralized CancellationTokenSource that can be used to coordinate graceful shutdown and cancellation across all components of the bot. MarketData

api/BacklashDTOs.Configuration.ErrorHand.summary: Class ErrorHandlerConfig Configuration settings for the CentralErrorHandler component that manages error processing, catastrophic failure detection, and internet connectivity monitoring. Inheritance object ErrorHandlerConfig Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: BacklashDTOs.Configuration Assembly: BacklashDTOs.dll Syntax public class ErrorHandlerConfig Remarks This configuration allows customization of error handling behavior including: Time window for error frequency monitoring Threshold for triggering catastrophic failure detection Parameters for internet connectivity check retry logic Properties | Edit this page View Source ErrorThreshold Gets or sets the threshold number of non-catastrophic errors within the monitoring window that triggers catastrophic failure detection and system restart. Declaration public int ErrorThreshold { get; set; } Property Value Type Description int Default is 10 errors. | Edit this page View Source ErrorWindowMinutes Gets or sets the time window in minutes for monitoring error frequency. Errors occurring within this window are counted toward the catastrophic threshold. Declaration public int ErrorWindowMinutes { get; set; } Property Value Type Description int Default is 5 minutes. | Edit this page View Source InternetCheckInitialDelayMs Gets or sets the initial delay in milliseconds between internet connectivity check attempts. This delay doubles with each retry attempt until reaching the maximum delay. Declaration public int InternetCheckInitialDelayMs { get; set; } Property Value Type Description int Default is 1000 milliseconds (1 second). | Edit this page View Source InternetCheckMaxAttempts Gets or sets the maximum number of attempts for internet connectivity checks before declaring the connection as failed. Declaration public int InternetCheckMaxAttempts { get; set; } Property Value Type Description int Default is 100 attempts. | Edit this page View Source InternetCheckMaxDelayMs Gets or sets the maximum delay in milliseconds between internet connectivity check attempts. The delay will not exceed this value even after exponential backoff. Declaration public int InternetCheckMaxDelayMs { get; set; } Property Value Type Description int Default is 60000 milliseconds (60 seconds).

api/BacklashDTOs.Configuration.InterestS.title: Class InterestScoreConfig | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashDTOs.Configuration.InterestS.summary: Class InterestScoreConfig Configuration class for InterestScoreService settings. Controls cache duration, performance monitoring, and operational parameters for the interest scoring system. These settings can be configured via appsettings.json and are injected through dependency injection. Inheritance object InterestScoreConfig Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: BacklashDTOs.Configuration Assembly: BacklashDTOs.dll Syntax public class InterestScoreConfig Properties | Edit this page View Source CacheDurationHours Gets or sets the cache duration in hours for percentile thresholds and market values. Default is 6 hours. Increasing this reduces database queries but may use stale data. Lower values provide more current data but increase computational overhead. Declaration public int CacheDurationHours { get; set; } Property Value Type Description int | Edit this page View Source EnablePerformanceMetrics Gets or sets whether to enable performance metrics collection. When enabled, tracks scoring operation times, cache hit rates, and other performance indicators. Disabling this improves performance but removes monitoring capabilities. Declaration public bool EnablePerformanceMetrics { get; set; } Property Value Type Description bool | Edit this page View Source MaxPerformanceMetricsHistory Gets or sets the maximum number of performance metrics to retain in memory. Older metrics are discarded when this limit is reached to prevent memory leaks. Higher values provide better historical analysis but consume more memory. Declaration public int MaxPerformanceMetricsHistory { get; set; } Property Value Type Description int

api/BacklashDTOs.KalshiAPI.html.summary: Namespace BacklashDTOs.KalshiAPI Classes APICandlestick Represents a single candlestick data point for a Kalshi market, including price and volume info. AnnouncementApi AnnouncementResponse ApiError BalanceResponse BatchOrderResponseItem BatchOrdersRequest BatchOrdersResponse CancelOrderResponse CandlestickResponse Represents a response containing candlestick data for a specific market on Kalshi. CreateOrderRequest CreateOrderResponse DeleteBatchOrderResponseItem DeleteOrdersBatchRequest DeleteOrdersBatchResponse EventMetadataResponse EventPosition Represents a user's aggregated position for an event across multiple markets on Kalshi. EventResponse ExchangeSchedule ExchangeScheduleResponse ExchangeStatus Represents the operational status of the Kalshi exchange and trading system. Fill FillsResponse IncentiveProgram IncentiveProgramsResponse KalshiEvent KalshiMarket Represents a market on the Kalshi platform with details such as ticker, prices, and trading status. KalshiSeries MaintenanceWindowApi MarketPositionApi Represents a user's position in a specific Kalshi market, including trading stats and profit/loss. MarketResponse Represents a response containing a list of Kalshi markets and pagination info. OrderApi Represents an order placed on the Kalshi platform with details about its status and execution. OrderDetails OrderQueuePositionResponse OrderResponse OrderbookMessage Represents a message containing order book data, either as a snapshot or delta update. OrdersResponse PositionsResponse Represents a response containing market and event position data. PriceData Represents price-related data for a market or event. PriceLevel Represents a price level in the order book with price and resting contract details. Declaration public double ExpectedFees { get; } Property Value Type Description double Remarks Calculated using the formula: fees = roundup(0.07 * C * P * (1-P)), where C is the number of contracts (absolute position size), P is the liquidation price in dollars, and roundup rounds to the next cent. Fees are summed across price levels in the order book when available, or based on the current bid (for "yes" positions) or ask (for "no" positions) price if no orders exist. Only applied for takers, not makers. Sourced from MarketData.RefreshPositionMetadata. Returns 0 if no position exists, position size is 0, or data is unavailable. | Edit this page View Source FeesPaid Gets or sets the total fees paid for trades in dollars. api/BacklashInterfaces.Enums.Subscriptio.title: Enum SubscriptionState | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashInterfaces.Enums.Subscriptio.summary: Enum SubscriptionState Represents the current state of a subscription to a specific market data channel via WebSocket. This enum is used by the SubscriptionManager to track and manage the lifecycle of subscriptions, ensuring proper handling of connection states and preventing duplicate or invalid operations. Namespace: BacklashInterfaces.Enums Assembly: BacklashInterfaces.dll Syntax public enum SubscriptionState Fields Name Description Subscribed The subscription is active and confirmed. Data for the channel is being received and processed. This state indicates a successful connection and ongoing data flow. Subscribing A subscription request has been sent to the server, but confirmation has not yet been received. This transitional state prevents multiple concurrent subscription attempts for the same channel. Unsubscribed The subscription is not active. This is the initial state before any subscription attempt, or after a successful unsubscription. In this state, no data is being received for the channel. Unsubscribing An unsubscription request has been sent to the server, but confirmation has not yet been received. This transitional state ensures clean disconnection and prevents new subscription attempts during teardown.

api/KalshiBotAPI.KalshiAPI.KalshiAPIServ.title: Class KalshiAPIService | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/KalshiBotAPI.KalshiAPI.KalshiAPIServ.summary: Class KalshiAPIService Provides a comprehensive service for interacting with the Kalshi trading API. This service handles authentication, market data retrieval, order management, position tracking, and various other API operations required for automated trading on the Kalshi platform. It implements the IKalshiAPIService interface and uses RSA-based authentication with API keys. The service maintains execution time tracking for performance monitoring, supports parallel processing for high-volume data operations, and includes configurable parameters for lookback periods and calculations. Robust error handling with cancellation token support throughout all operations. api/KalshiBotAPI.KalshiAPI.html.title: Namespace KalshiBotAPI.KalshiAPI | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/KalshiBotAPI.KalshiAPI.html.summary: Namespace KalshiBotAPI.KalshiAPI Classes KalshiAPIService Provides a comprehensive service for interacting with the Kalshi trading API. This service handles authentication, market data retrieval, order management, position tracking, and various other API operations required for automated trading on the Kalshi platform. It implements the IKalshiAPIService interface and uses RSA-based authentication with API keys. The service maintains execution time tracking for performance monitoring, supports parallel processing for high-volume data operations, and includes configurable parameters for lookback periods and calculations. Robust error handling with cancellation token support throughout all operations.

api/KalshiBotAPI.Websockets.KalshiWebSoc.summary: Class KalshiWebSocketClient Main WebSocket client for connecting to Kalshi's trading platform. Manages the complete lifecycle of WebSocket connections, subscriptions to market data channels, and real-time message processing. Acts as the central orchestrator between connection management, subscription handling, and message processing components. Inheritance object KalshiWebSocketClient Implements IKalshiWebSocketClient Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: KalshiBotAPI.Websockets Assembly: KalshiBotAPI.dll Syntax public class KalshiWebSocketClient : IKalshiWebSocketClient Remarks This class implements the IKalshiWebSocketClient interface and coordinates with: IWebSocketConnectionManager: Handles low-level WebSocket connection and reconnection ISubscriptionManager: Manages channel subscriptions and subscription states IMessageProcessor: Processes incoming WebSocket messages and raises events IDataCache: Provides access to watched markets and other cached data ISqlDataService: Handles database operations for market data persistence IStatusTrackerService: Provides cancellation tokens and status tracking IBotReadyStatus: Tracks bot readiness state Constructors | Edit this page View Source KalshiWebSocketClient(IOptions<KalshiConfig>, ILogger<IKalshiWebSocketClient>, IStatusTrackerService, IBotReadyStatus, ISqlDataService, IWebSocketConnectionManager, ISubscriptionManager, IMessageProcessor, IDataCache, bool, int) Initializes a new instance of the KalshiWebSocketClient class. api/KalshiBotAPI.Websockets.MessageProce.summary: Class MessageProcessor Processes incoming WebSocket messages from Kalshi's trading platform, routing them to appropriate handlers based on message type. Features configurable message batching for high-volume scenarios, performance metrics collection, sophisticated locking mechanisms, and enhanced message deduplication with warnings. Manages event counting, order book message queuing, and integrates with data persistence and API services for comprehensive market data processing.
