api/KalshiBotAPI.Websockets.WebSocketCon.title: Class WebSocketConnectionManager | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/KalshiBotAPI.Websockets.WebSocketCon.summary: Class WebSocketConnectionManager Manages WebSocket connections to Kalshi's trading platform with robust reconnection logic, authentication, and thread-safe operations. This class handles the low-level WebSocket communication layer, providing reliable connection management for real-time market data streaming. Inheritance object WebSocketConnectionManager Implements IWebSocketConnectionManager Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: KalshiBotAPI.Websockets Assembly: KalshiBotAPI.dll Syntax public class WebSocketConnectionManager : IWebSocketConnectionManager Remarks The WebSocketConnectionManager is responsible for: - Establishing authenticated WebSocket connections using RSA-based signatures - Managing connection lifecycle with automatic reconnection capabilities - Providing thread-safe message sending and receiving operations - Handling connection state monitoring and error recovery - Coordinating with higher-level components for message processing This class implements the IWebSocketConnectionManager interface and is designed to be used by higher-level orchestration components like KalshiWebSocketClient. It provides the foundation for reliable real-time communication with Kalshi's trading platform. Key features include: - Configurable retry logic with customizable delays and maximum attempts - Performance metrics collection (connection success rates, latency, message throughput) - Signature caching for reduced computational overhead with hit rate tracking - Configurable WebSocket buffer sizes with utilization monitoring - Semaphore-based synchronization to prevent concurrent connection attempts - Configurable reconnection behavior (can be disabled/enabled) - Connection failure reason tracking and diagnostics - Sliding window throughput calculation for accurate real-time metrics - Comprehensive logging for operational visibility - Proper resource cleanup and disposal Constructors | Edit this page View Source WebSocketConnectionManager(IOptions<KalshiConfig>, ILogger<WebSocketConnectionManager>) Initializes a new instance of the WebSocketConnectionManager class. Declaration public WebSocketConnectionManager(IOptions<KalshiConfig> kalshiConfig, ILogger<WebSocketConnectionManager> logger) Parameters Type Name Description IOptions<KalshiConfig> kalshiConfig Configuration options containing API credentials and connection settings. ILogger<WebSocketConnectionManager> logger Logger instance for recording connection operations and errors. Remarks The constructor sets up the RSA private key for authentication by loading it from the configured key file. This key is used to generate signatures for WebSocket connection authentication with Kalshi's servers. It also initializes all configurable parameters including retry delays, buffer sizes, timeouts, and cache durations for optimal performance. Properties | Edit this page View Source AverageBufferUtilization Gets the average buffer utilization (0.0 to 1.0). Declaration public double AverageBufferUtilization { get; } Property Value Type Description double Remarks Indicates how efficiently the WebSocket buffer is being used. Values closer to 1.0 suggest the buffer size is appropriate for the message sizes. Values much lower than 1.0 might indicate the buffer is oversized. | Edit this page View Source AverageConnectionLatency Gets the average connection latency in milliseconds. Declaration public double AverageConnectionLatency { get; } Property Value Type Description double | Edit this page View Source ConnectSemaphoreCount Gets the current count of the connection semaphore, indicating connection operation status. Declaration public int ConnectSemaphoreCount { get; } Property Value Type Description int Remarks The semaphore count indicates whether connection operations are currently in progress: - 1: No connection operation is active (semaphore is available) - 0: A connection operation is currently in progress (semaphore is held) This property is useful for monitoring and debugging connection concurrency issues. A count of 0 indicates that another thread is currently attempting to connect or reset the WebSocket connection. | Edit this page View Source ConnectionAttempts Gets the total number of connection attempts made. Declaration public int ConnectionAttempts { get; } Property Value Type Description int | Edit this page View Source ConnectionFailureReasons Gets the connection failure reasons and their counts. Declaration public IReadOnlyDictionary<string, int> ConnectionFailureReasons { get; } Property Value Type Description IReadOnlyDictionary<string, int> Remarks Provides detailed breakdown of failure types (e.g., "WebSocketException", "TimeoutException") to help diagnose connection issues and optimize retry strategies. | Edit this page View Source ConnectionFailures Gets the total number of connection failures. Declaration public int ConnectionFailures { get; } Property Value Type Description int Remarks Tracks all connection failures for monitoring connection reliability and identifying potential network or configuration issues. | Edit this page View Source ConnectionSuccessRate Gets the connection success rate (0.0 to 1.0). Declaration public double ConnectionSuccessRate { get; } Property Value Type Description double | Edit this page View Source ConnectionSuccesses Gets the number of successful connections. Declaration public int ConnectionSuccesses { get; } Property Value Type Description int | Edit this page View Source MessageThroughput Gets the current message throughput (messages per second). Declaration public double MessageThroughput { get; } Property Value Type Description double Remarks Calculated using a sliding window approach over the last 60 seconds for more accurate real-time throughput measurement. | Edit this page View Source MessagesReceived Gets the total number of messages received. Declaration public int MessagesReceived { get; } Property Value Type Description int | Edit this page View Source ReconnectionCount Gets the number of reconnections performed. Declaration public int ReconnectionCount { get; } Property Value Type Description int | Edit this page View Source SignatureCacheHitRate Gets the signature cache hit rate (0.0 to 1.0). Declaration public double SignatureCacheHitRate { get; } Property Value Type Description double Remarks Indicates the effectiveness of signature caching in reducing computational overhead. Higher values suggest better cache performance and reduced CPU usage for authentication. | Edit this page View Source SignatureCacheHits Gets the total number of signature cache hits. Declaration public int SignatureCacheHits { get; } Property Value Type Description int Remarks Tracks successful cache lookups that avoided expensive signature generation. | Edit this page View Source SignatureCacheMisses Gets the total number of signature cache misses. Declaration public int SignatureCacheMisses { get; } Property Value Type Description int Remarks Tracks cache misses that required new signature generation. | Edit this page View Source TotalBytesReceived Gets the total bytes received. Declaration public long TotalBytesReceived { get; } Property Value Type Description long Remarks Cumulative count of all data received through the WebSocket connection. Useful for monitoring data transfer volumes and connection efficiency. Methods | Edit this page View Source ConnectAsync(int) Establishes a WebSocket connection to Kalshi's trading platform with automatic retry logic. Declaration public Task ConnectAsync(int retryCount = 0) Parameters Type Name Description int retryCount The current retry attempt number (used internally for exponential backoff). Returns Type Description Task A task representing the asynchronous connection operation. Remarks This method handles the complete WebSocket connection process including: - Checking reconnection permissions and current connection state - Acquiring connection semaphore to prevent concurrent connection attempts - Disposing of stale connections and creating new WebSocket instances - Setting up authentication headers with RSA signature generation - Establishing the connection with proper error handling The method implements configurable retry logic for connection failures, with configurable retry delays and maximum attempts. If all retries fail, it throws an InvalidOperationException with details about the connection failure. Thread Safety: This method is thread-safe and uses semaphores to prevent concurrent connection attempts that could cause race conditions. Exceptions Type Condition InvalidOperationException Thrown when the WebSocket connection cannot be established after all retry attempts. | Edit this page View Source DisableReconnect() Disables automatic reconnection for the WebSocket connection. Declaration public void DisableReconnect() Remarks When reconnection is disabled, the WebSocket will not attempt to automatically reconnect after connection failures. This is useful during application shutdown or when manual connection control is required. The current connection state remains unchanged until explicitly modified. | Edit this page View Source EnableReconnect() Enables automatic reconnection for the WebSocket connection. Declaration public void EnableReconnect() Remarks When reconnection is enabled, the WebSocket will automatically attempt to reconnect after connection failures using configurable retry logic. This is the default behavior for maintaining reliable connections to Kalshi's platform. | Edit this page View Source GetWebSocket() Gets the current WebSocket instance for direct access. Declaration public ClientWebSocket? GetWebSocket() Returns Type Description ClientWebSocket The current ClientWebSocket instance, or null if not connected. Remarks This method provides direct access to the underlying WebSocket for advanced operations that require low-level WebSocket manipulation. The returned instance should be used with caution as it bypasses the connection manager's thread-safety mechanisms. Thread Safety: This method is thread-safe and uses proper locking to ensure the WebSocket instance is not modified while being accessed. | Edit this page View Source IsConnected() Determines whether the WebSocket connection is currently active and ready for communication. Declaration public bool IsConnected() Returns Type Description bool True if the WebSocket is connected and in an open state, false otherwise. Remarks This method performs a comprehensive connection check by evaluating: - The internal connection flag (_isConnected) - The existence of a WebSocket instance - The WebSocket's current state (must be WebSocketState.Open) The method is thread-safe and provides detailed logging of the connection status for debugging purposes. It should be used to verify connection state before attempting to send messages or perform connection-dependent operations. | Edit this page View Source ReceiveAsync() Continuously receives messages from the WebSocket connection until the connection is closed or an error occurs. Declaration public Task ReceiveAsync() Returns Type Description Task A task representing the asynchronous receive operation. Remarks This method implements a continuous message receiving loop that: - Monitors the connection state and terminates if the WebSocket is no longer connected - Receives message fragments and assembles them into complete messages - Handles WebSocket close messages by throwing an exception to trigger reconnection - Processes only text messages, logging errors for unexpected message types - Maintains message ordering by waiting for EndOfMessage before processing The method includes comprehensive error handling for: - Connection loss during message reception - Server-initiated connection closures - Unexpected message types - General exceptions during the receive process Note: In the current implementation, received messages are logged but not processed. Message processing is handled by the higher-level MessageProcessor component that should be called from the main WebSocket client orchestrator. Exceptions Type Condition InvalidOperationException Thrown when the WebSocket connection is lost or closed by the server. | Edit this page View Source ResetConnectionAsync() Resets the current WebSocket connection and establishes a new one. Declaration public Task ResetConnectionAsync() Returns Type Description Task A task representing the asynchronous reset operation. Remarks This method performs a clean reset of the WebSocket connection by: - Checking if a reconnection is already in progress to prevent duplicate operations - Closing the existing connection gracefully if it exists - Disposing of the old WebSocket instance - Establishing a new connection if reconnection is allowed The method includes proper error handling and ensures thread-safe operations during the connection reset process. If reconnection is disabled, the method will only clean up the existing connection without establishing a new one. | Edit this page View Source SendMessageAsync(string) Sends a text message through the WebSocket connection. Declaration public Task SendMessageAsync(string message) Parameters Type Name Description string message The message string to send. Returns Type Description Task A task representing the asynchronous send operation. Remarks This method ensures thread-safe message sending by: - Acquiring the WebSocket lock to prevent concurrent access - Verifying the connection is active before sending - Converting the message to UTF-8 bytes for transmission - Sending the message as a complete text frame If the WebSocket is not connected or in an invalid state, the method will throw an InvalidOperationException with details about the connection status. Exceptions Type Condition InvalidOperationException Thrown when attempting to send a message while the WebSocket is not connected. | Edit this page View Source StopAsync() Gracefully stops the WebSocket connection and disables reconnection. Declaration public Task StopAsync() Returns Type Description Task A task representing the asynchronous stop operation. Remarks This method performs a clean shutdown by: - Disabling automatic reconnection to prevent restart attempts - Closing the active WebSocket connection with a normal closure status - Disposing of the WebSocket instance to free resources - Updating the connection state to reflect the shutdown The method includes proper error handling to ensure the shutdown process completes even if individual steps fail. This is typically called during application shutdown or when manual connection control is required. Implements IWebSocketConnectionManager

api/KalshiBotAPI.Websockets.html.title: Namespace KalshiBotAPI.Websockets | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/KalshiBotAPI.Websockets.html.summary: Namespace KalshiBotAPI.Websockets Classes KalshiWebSocketClient Main WebSocket client for connecting to Kalshi's trading platform. Manages the complete lifecycle of WebSocket connections, subscriptions to market data channels, and real-time message processing. Acts as the central orchestrator between connection management, subscription handling, and message processing components. MessageProcessor Processes incoming WebSocket messages from Kalshi's trading platform, routing them to appropriate handlers based on message type. Features configurable message batching for high-volume scenarios, performance metrics collection, sophisticated locking mechanisms, and enhanced message deduplication with warnings. Manages event counting, order book message queuing, and integrates with data persistence and API services for comprehensive market data processing. SubscriptionManager Manages WebSocket channel subscriptions for real-time market data from Kalshi's trading platform. Handles subscription lifecycle, state tracking, confirmation processing, and queue management for reliable market data streaming with proper error handling and recovery mechanisms. WebSocketConnectionManager Manages WebSocket connections to Kalshi's trading platform with robust reconnection logic, authentication, and thread-safe operations. This class handles the low-level WebSocket communication layer, providing reliable connection management for real-time market data streaming.

api/KalshiBotData.Data.SqlDataService.ht.title: Class SqlDataService | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/KalshiBotData.Data.SqlDataService.ht.summary: Class SqlDataService Provides asynchronous data persistence services for real-time market data from Kalshi's trading platform. This service manages concurrent queues for different data types (order book, trades, fills, lifecycle events) and processes them using configurable background worker tasks with retry logic for transient SQL errors. Supports batch processing, performance metrics collection, input validation, and configurable queue sizes to prevent resource exhaustion. Implements the ISqlDataService interface for dependency injection and proper resource management. Inheritance object SqlDataService Implements ISqlDataService IDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: KalshiBotData.Data Assembly: KalshiBotData.dll Syntax public class SqlDataService : ISqlDataService, IDisposable Remarks The service uses Polly for retry policies on SQL operations and maintains separate queues to ensure data integrity and prevent blocking between different data types. All operations are asynchronous to support high-throughput real-time data processing without impacting the main application flow. Configuration options include retry counts, delays, queue sizes, worker counts, and batch sizes. Performance metrics are collected for processing rates, queue depths, and success rates. Input validation ensures JSON data integrity before queuing operations. Constructors | Edit this page View Source SqlDataService(IConfiguration, ILogger<ISqlDataService>) Initializes a new instance of the SqlDataService with configuration and logging dependencies. Sets up concurrent queues for different data types and starts background worker tasks for processing. ILogger<ISqlDataService> logger Logger for recording service operations and errors. api/KalshiBotData.Data.html.title: Namespace KalshiBotData.Data | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/KalshiBotData.Data.html.summary: Namespace KalshiBotData.Data Classes KalshiBotContext Entity Framework DbContext implementation for the Kalshi trading bot system. Provides comprehensive data access layer for managing trading entities including markets, events, series, snapshots, brain instances, and various trading-related data. Implements IKalshiBotContext interface for dependency injection and testing. SqlDataService Provides asynchronous data persistence services for real-time market data from Kalshi's trading platform. This service manages concurrent queues for different data types (order book, trades, fills, lifecycle events) and processes them using configurable background worker tasks with retry logic for transient SQL errors. Supports batch processing, performance metrics collection, input validation, and configurable queue sizes to prevent resource exhaustion. Implements the ISqlDataService interface for dependency injection and proper resource management.

This entity manages the settings and runtime state for individual brain instances that execute trading strategies, monitor markets, and capture snapshots. Each brain instance can have different configurations for market watching, position tracking, and resource usage. This entity tracks the progression of an event from opening to settlement, including all critical timestamps and state changes. It serves as the authoritative source for event status and timing used throughout the trading bot for market state management and strategy execution. api/KalshiBotData.Models.html.title: Namespace KalshiBotData.Models | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/KalshiBotData.Models.html.summary: Namespace KalshiBotData.Models Classes Announcement Represents a system announcement or notification in the Kalshi trading bot ecosystem. This entity stores important messages, alerts, or updates that need to be communicated to users or other system components. Announcements can have different types and statuses to categorize their importance and delivery state. BrainInstance Represents a brain instance configuration and operational state in the trading bot system. This entity manages the settings and runtime state for individual brain instances that execute trading strategies, monitor markets, and capture snapshots. Each brain instance can have different configurations for market watching, position tracking, and resource usage. BrainPersistenceEntity Database entity for storing BrainPersistence data. Uses JSON serialization to store complex nested objects. Candlestick Event EventLifecycle Represents the complete lifecycle state and timing information for a trading event in the Kalshi system. This entity tracks the progression of an event from opening to settlement, including all critical timestamps and state changes. It serves as the authoritative source for event status and timing used throughout the trading bot for market state management and strategy execution. ExchangeSchedule Represents the operational schedule and maintenance configuration for the Kalshi exchange. This entity manages the overall timing and availability of the trading platform, including regular operating hours and scheduled maintenance periods. It serves as the master schedule that coordinates all trading activities and system maintenance across the platform. Fill Represents a trade execution (fill) in the Kalshi trading system. This entity captures the details of individual trade executions, including pricing, quantity, and market information. Fills are generated when orders are matched and executed in the market, providing a complete audit trail of all trading activity. LogEntry Represents a structured log entry in the Kalshi trading bot system. This entity captures comprehensive logging information including severity levels, contextual data, and system state for debugging and monitoring purposes. Log entries are used throughout the system for troubleshooting, performance monitoring, and maintaining an audit trail of system activities. MaintenanceWindow Represents a scheduled maintenance window for the Kalshi exchange. This entity defines periods when the exchange is unavailable for trading due to planned maintenance, upgrades, or system updates. Maintenance windows are critical for coordinating system downtime and ensuring trading bots can react appropriately to planned service interruptions. Market Represents a tradable market with comprehensive pricing, volume, and status information. This entity serves as the core data model for all market-related operations in the trading bot, containing real-time and historical market data, order book information, and market metadata. Maps to the Markets database table and is central to the trading system's data model. MarketPosition MarketType Represents a category or type classification for markets in the Kalshi trading system. This entity provides a way to group and categorize different types of trading markets based on their characteristics, such as binary outcome markets, range markets, or other specialized market types. Market types help in filtering, analysis, and applying different trading strategies based on market characteristics. MarketWatch Represents the watch status and monitoring configuration for a specific market. This entity tracks which markets are being actively monitored by the trading bot, including interest scores, brain instance assignments, and activity metrics. MarketWatch entities are crucial for managing the bot's market coverage and optimizing resource allocation across different trading opportunities. Order Represents a trading order in the Kalshi system with comprehensive execution tracking. This entity captures all aspects of an order's lifecycle including pricing, status, execution details, and performance metrics. Orders are the fundamental unit of trading activity and this entity provides complete visibility into order management and execution. OverseerLogEntry Represents a log entry from the overseer system for monitoring and debugging. This entity stores detailed logging information from the overseer component, including error details, environment context, and session tracking. Maps to the t_OverseerLogs database table. Series SeriesSettlementSource Represents a settlement source reference for a trading series. This entity links a series to external data sources that will be used to determine the final outcome and settlement value of the series contracts. Settlement sources provide the authoritative data that resolves the market and determines payouts to winning positions. SeriesTag Represents a tag or category label associated with a trading series. This entity provides a flexible tagging system for categorizing and organizing trading series based on various characteristics such as market type, theme, or other classification criteria. Tags enable efficient filtering and discovery of series with similar attributes. SignalRClient Represents a client connected to the system via SignalR for real-time communication. This entity tracks client connections, authentication, and connection state for managing real-time data distribution to dashboards, overseer systems, and other connected clients. It enables secure, authenticated real-time communication between the trading bot system and its clients. Snapshot Represents a comprehensive snapshot of market state and trading activity at a specific point in time. This entity captures detailed market data including pricing, volume, velocity metrics, and order book information for analysis, backtesting, and trading strategy evaluation. Snapshots serve as the primary data structure for understanding market behavior and making informed trading decisions. SnapshotGroup SnapshotSchema Represents the schema definition and versioning for market snapshots. This entity defines the structure and format of snapshot data, ensuring consistency and compatibility across different versions of the system. Schema definitions are crucial for data validation, migration, and maintaining backward compatibility as the snapshot format evolves. StandardHours Represents the standard operating hours configuration for the Kalshi exchange. This entity defines the regular trading schedule and time periods when the exchange is open for business. Standard hours are used to coordinate trading activities, schedule maintenance, and ensure proper market operation timing across the platform. StandardHoursSession Represents a specific session within the standard operating hours of the Kalshi exchange. This entity defines individual time periods on specific days when trading is active. Sessions allow for flexible scheduling where different days may have different trading windows, and multiple sessions can exist within a single day's operating hours. StratData Represents strategy configuration and metadata for trading strategies in the Kalshi bot system. This entity stores the definition and parameters of trading strategies, allowing for flexible strategy management and execution. Strategies can be stored as JSON configurations and instantiated dynamically based on market conditions and trading requirements. Ticker Represents real-time ticker data for a Kalshi market, capturing price and volume information at a specific point in time. This entity stores the most recent market data including bid/ask prices, trading volume, and open interest. Ticker data is crucial for real-time trading decisions and market analysis, providing the current state of market pricing and liquidity for individual contracts. Trade Represents a completed trade execution in the Kalshi market. This entity captures the details of individual trades including pricing, quantity, and market information. Trades are the fundamental records of market activity and provide the basis for volume analysis, price discovery, and market trend identification. WeightSet Represents a set of trading weights for portfolio optimization and strategy allocation. This entity defines how trading capital and resources should be distributed across different markets and strategies. Weight sets enable sophisticated portfolio management by allowing dynamic allocation based on market conditions, risk parameters, and performance metrics. WeightSetMarket Represents the market-specific allocation and performance data within a weight set. This entity defines how individual markets are weighted within a portfolio strategy, tracking both the allocation percentage and the realized performance. It enables granular control over portfolio composition and performance attribution at the market level.

api/KalshiBotLogging.DatabaseLogger.html.summary: Class DatabaseLogger Custom logger implementation that integrates with the KalshiBot logging infrastructure. api/KalshiBotOverseer.Overseer.html.title: Class Overseer | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/KalshiBotOverseer.Overseer.html.summary: Class Overseer Central orchestrator for the KalshiBot Overseer system that manages WebSocket connections, handles real-time market data events, coordinates periodic data fetching, and maintains system health monitoring. This class serves as the main entry point for overseer operations, integrating with WebSocket clients, database services, and SignalR hubs to provide comprehensive monitoring and control of the trading bot ecosystem. Key features: Configurable API fetch intervals (default: 10 minutes) Configurable system info logging intervals (default: 1 minute) Performance metrics collection for WebSocket events and API operations Input validation for event data processing to prevent null reference exceptions Progress reporting for long-running API fetch operations Batch processing for brain persistence logging (default batch size: 50) Configurable SignalR broadcast batch sizes (default: 10) Inheritance object Overseer Implements IDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: KalshiBotOverseer Assembly: KalshiBotOverseer.dll Syntax public class Overseer : IDisposable Constructors | Edit this page View Source Overseer(IKalshiWebSocketClient, IServiceScopeFactory, ILogger<Overseer>, IHubContext<OverseerHub>, IOptions<OverseerConfig>) Initializes a new instance of the Overseer class with required dependencies. Declaration public Overseer(IKalshiWebSocketClient webSocketClient, IServiceScopeFactory scopeFactory, ILogger<Overseer> logger, IHubContext<OverseerHub> hubContext, IOptions<OverseerConfig> config) Parameters Type Name Description IKalshiWebSocketClient webSocketClient WebSocket client for real-time market data streaming. IServiceScopeFactory scopeFactory Factory for creating service scopes for dependency injection. ILogger<Overseer> logger Logger for recording system events and diagnostics. IHubContext<OverseerHub> hubContext SignalR hub context for real-time client communication. IOptions<OverseerConfig> config Configuration options for the overseer system. Methods | Edit this page View Source Dispose() Disposes of the overseer and cleans up all resources including timers and event subscriptions. Declaration public void Dispose() | Edit this page View Source Start() Starts the overseer system by initializing logging, subscribing to WebSocket events, and beginning periodic data fetching and system monitoring operations. Declaration public Task Start() Returns Type Description Task A task representing the asynchronous operation. | Edit this page View Source StartApiDataFetchTimer() Starts periodic fetching of announcements and exchange schedule data at the configured interval. This ensures the system stays synchronized with Kalshi's API data. The interval is configurable via appsettings.json (OverseerConfig.ApiFetchIntervalMinutes). api/KalshiBotOverseer.Services.BrainPers.summary: Class BrainPersistenceService Service responsible for managing brain instance states with configurable persistence options. This service provides thread-safe operations for storing, retrieving, and updating brain configuration, market watch lists, and performance metrics history. It supports both in-memory storage and optional database persistence for recovery after application restarts. The service includes configurable history retention, performance metrics collection, batch operations, and comprehensive health monitoring. api/KalshiBotOverseer.State.OverseerRead.title: Class OverseerReadyStatus | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/KalshiBotOverseer.State.OverseerRead.summary: Class OverseerReadyStatus Manages the readiness status of different components within the Kalshi overseer system. This class provides TaskCompletionSource objects to signal when various parts of the overseer have completed their initialization or are ready for operation, with comprehensive logging and metrics. Inheritance object OverseerReadyStatus Implements IBotReadyStatus Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: KalshiBotOverseer.State Assembly: KalshiBotOverseer.dll Syntax public class OverseerReadyStatus : IBotReadyStatus Remarks The readiness status system allows different components to signal their completion state: InitializationCompleted: Signals when the overseer's core initialization is finished BrowserReady: Signals when browser-related components are ready (if applicable) Components can await these TaskCompletionSource objects to coordinate startup sequences, and can set the results to signal completion to waiting components. Features include: Comprehensive logging for all state changes and operations Input validation to prevent null reference exceptions Configurable default states through OverseerReadyConfig Metrics collection for readiness timing and operation performance Constructors | Edit this page View Source OverseerReadyStatus(ILogger<OverseerReadyStatus>, IOptions<OverseerReadyConfig>) Initializes a new instance of the OverseerReadyStatus class. Creates the initial TaskCompletionSource objects and sets up the default state. Declaration public OverseerReadyStatus(ILogger<OverseerReadyStatus> logger, IOptions<OverseerReadyConfig> config) Parameters Type Name Description ILogger<OverseerReadyStatus> logger The logger for tracking readiness state changes. IOptions<OverseerReadyConfig> config The configuration for default readiness states. Properties | Edit this page View Source BrowserReady Gets or sets the TaskCompletionSource that signals when browser-related components are ready. This is used for components that depend on browser initialization or UI readiness. Declaration public TaskCompletionSource<bool> BrowserReady { get; set; } Property Value Type Description TaskCompletionSource<bool> The TaskCompletionSource for browser readiness signaling. | Edit this page View Source InitializationCompleted Gets or sets the TaskCompletionSource that signals when the overseer's core initialization is complete. Components can await this task to wait for initialization to finish, or set its result to signal completion. Declaration public TaskCompletionSource<bool> InitializationCompleted { get; set; } Property Value Type Description TaskCompletionSource<bool> The TaskCompletionSource for initialization completion signaling. Methods | Edit this page View Source ResetAll() Resets all readiness status indicators to their initial state. This creates new TaskCompletionSource objects and sets the initialization status to the configured default. Declaration public void ResetAll() Remarks This method is typically called when restarting the overseer or when a reset is needed to clear all previous readiness signals and start fresh. Implements IBotReadyStatus

api/KalshiBotOverseer.State.OverseerStat.title: Class OverseerStatusTracker | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/KalshiBotOverseer.State.OverseerStat.summary: Class OverseerStatusTracker Manages the global cancellation state for the Kalshi overseer system. This class provides a centralized CancellationTokenSource that can be used to coordinate graceful shutdown and cancellation across all components of the overseer. Inheritance object OverseerStatusTracker Implements IStatusTrackerService IDisposable IAsyncDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: KalshiBotOverseer.State Assembly: KalshiBotOverseer.dll Syntax public class OverseerStatusTracker : IStatusTrackerService, IDisposable, IAsyncDisposable Remarks The status tracker ensures thread-safe access to the global cancellation token and provides: A single CancellationToken that all components can monitor for shutdown signals Thread-safe cancellation operations with comprehensive logging Proper resource cleanup through IDisposable and IAsyncDisposable implementations Reset capability for restarting the cancellation state with timing metrics Metrics collection for cancellation frequency and operation timing This is registered as a singleton service to ensure all components share the same cancellation state. Constructors | Edit this page View Source OverseerStatusTracker(ILogger<OverseerStatusTracker>) Initializes a new instance of the OverseerStatusTracker class. Creates the initial CancellationTokenSource and sets up the cancellation state with logging and metrics. Declaration public OverseerStatusTracker(ILogger<OverseerStatusTracker> logger) Parameters Type Name Description ILogger<OverseerStatusTracker> logger The logger for tracking cancellation operations and state changes. Methods | Edit this page View Source CancelAll() Cancels all operations by triggering the global cancellation token. This signals all components that are monitoring the cancellation token to stop their operations. Declaration public void CancelAll() Remarks This method is thread-safe and should be called when the overseer needs to shut down. After calling this method, the cancellation token will be in a cancelled state. | Edit this page View Source Dispose() Disposes of the resources used by the status tracker synchronously. This cancels any pending operations and disposes of the CancellationTokenSource and metrics. Declaration public void Dispose() Remarks This method should be called when the status tracker is no longer needed, typically during application shutdown. It ensures proper cleanup of unmanaged resources and logs the disposal operation for monitoring purposes. | Edit this page View Source DisposeAsync() Asynchronously disposes of the resources used by the status tracker. This cancels any pending operations and disposes of the CancellationTokenSource. Declaration public ValueTask DisposeAsync() Returns Type Description ValueTask A ValueTask representing the asynchronous dispose operation. | Edit this page View Source GetCancellationToken() Gets the global CancellationToken that all components should monitor for shutdown signals. Declaration public CancellationToken GetCancellationToken() Returns Type Description CancellationToken The CancellationToken that can be used to monitor for cancellation requests. Remarks This method provides thread-safe access to the cancellation token. Components should regularly check this token or use it in async operations to respond to shutdown requests gracefully. | Edit this page View Source ResetAll() Resets the cancellation state by creating a new CancellationTokenSource. This clears any previous cancellation state and allows the overseer to restart operations. Declaration public void ResetAll() Remarks This method properly disposes of the old CancellationTokenSource before creating a new one. It should be called when restarting the overseer or when a fresh cancellation state is needed. Implements IStatusTrackerService IDisposable IAsyncDisposable

api/KalshiBotOverseer.html.title: Namespace KalshiBotOverseer | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/KalshiBotOverseer.html.summary: Namespace KalshiBotOverseer Classes CheckInData Data structure containing comprehensive information about a brain instance's current state, used during periodic check-in operations to report status to the overseer system. OvernightActivitiesHelper Service class that handles overnight maintenance tasks for the Kalshi trading bot overseer. This includes refreshing market data, calculating interest scores, importing snapshots, cleaning up old watches, and generating snapshot groups for market analysis. Overseer Central orchestrator for the KalshiBot Overseer system that manages WebSocket connections, handles real-time market data events, coordinates periodic data fetching, and maintains system health monitoring. This class serves as the main entry point for overseer operations, integrating with WebSocket clients, database services, and SignalR hubs to provide comprehensive monitoring and control of the trading bot ecosystem. Key features: Configurable API fetch intervals (default: 10 minutes) Configurable system info logging intervals (default: 1 minute) Performance metrics collection for WebSocket events and API operations Input validation for event data processing to prevent null reference exceptions Progress reporting for long-running API fetch operations Batch processing for brain persistence logging (default batch size: 50) Configurable SignalR broadcast batch sizes (default: 10) OverseerConfig Configuration options for the Overseer system. OverseerHub SignalR hub that manages real-time communication between the Kalshi trading bot overseer and connected clients. This hub handles client connections, authentication via handshakes, periodic status check-ins from brain instances, and broadcasting of trading data and updates. It serves as the central communication point for the overseer system, enabling real-time monitoring and control of trading bot operations. OverseerHub.ClientInfo Represents information about a connected client, including identification, connection details, and last activity timestamp. OverseerHubConfig Configuration options for the OverseerHub SignalR hub. Startup WebSocketMonitorServiceLite

api/TradingSimulator.Simulator.Simulator.title: Class SimulatorReporting | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/TradingSimulator.Simulator.Simulator.summary: Class SimulatorReporting Provides comprehensive reporting and analysis functionality for trading simulator operations. This class serves as the core engine for detecting velocity discrepancies in market snapshots, computing rolling observations, generating detailed memos, and coalescing discrepancy data. It is designed to analyze orderbook flow patterns and identify potential anomalies in market data that could indicate trading opportunities or data quality issues. Key Features: Configurable analysis parameters via AnalysisConfiguration class Async processing support for better performance with large datasets Parallel processing for multiple market analyses Comprehensive input validation for data integrity Performance metrics collection and timing analysis Configurable logging with automatic directory creation Spike suppression to reduce false positives Robust error handling and recovery Inheritance object SimulatorReporting Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: TradingSimulator.Simulator Assembly: TradingSimulator.dll Syntax public class SimulatorReporting Constructors | Edit this page View Source SimulatorReporting(AnalysisConfiguration) Declaration public SimulatorReporting(AnalysisConfiguration config = null) Parameters Type Name Description AnalysisConfiguration config Methods | Edit this page View Source DetectVelocityDiscrepancies(List<MarketSnapshot>, bool) Detects velocity discrepancies in market snapshots by analyzing orderbook flow patterns. This method compares observed orderbook velocity changes against expected flows derived from rolling window analysis, identifying potential anomalies that may indicate trading opportunities or data quality issues. The analysis includes spike suppression, leakage tolerance, and various statistical thresholds to reduce false positives. Features: Automatic parallel processing for large datasets (>100 snapshots) Comprehensive input validation and error handling Performance metrics collection when enabled Configurable logging with automatic directory creation Robust spike suppression to reduce false positives Declaration public List<PricePoint> DetectVelocityDiscrepancies(List<MarketSnapshot> s, bool CreateLog) Parameters Type Name Description List<MarketSnapshot> s The list of market snapshots to analyze for velocity discrepancies. bool CreateLog Whether to create log files for detected discrepancies. Returns Type Description List<PricePoint> A list of PricePoint objects representing detected velocity discrepancies. | Edit this page View Source DetectVelocityDiscrepanciesAsync(List<MarketSnapshot>, bool) Asynchronously detects velocity discrepancies in market snapshots by analyzing orderbook flow patterns. This method provides the same functionality as the synchronous version but runs on a background thread to avoid blocking the calling thread. Ideal for large datasets or when responsiveness is critical. Declaration public Task<List<PricePoint>> DetectVelocityDiscrepanciesAsync(List<MarketSnapshot> s, bool CreateLog) Parameters Type Name Description List<MarketSnapshot> s The list of market snapshots to analyze for velocity discrepancies. bool CreateLog Whether to create log files for detected discrepancies. Returns Type Description Task<List<PricePoint>> A task that represents the asynchronous operation, containing a list of PricePoint objects representing detected velocity discrepancies. | Edit this page View Source GetPerformanceMetrics() Declaration public PerformanceMetrics GetPerformanceMetrics() Returns Type Description PerformanceMetrics

api/TradingSimulator.Strategies.TradingD.title: Class TradingDecision | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/TradingSimulator.Strategies.TradingD.summary: Class TradingDecision Represents a trading decision generated by a trading strategy evaluation. This class encapsulates the signals, metadata, and finality status of a decision made during backtesting or live trading simulation. It serves as the primary output container for strategy logic, allowing strategies to communicate actionable insights and supplementary information to the trading engine. Each signal is represented as a key-value pair where the key is the signal name (e.g., "PriceRise", "PriceDrop") and the value is a numeric strength or confidence level (typically 0.0 to 1.0, where 1.0 indicates strong signal). These signals are used by the trading engine to determine the appropriate trading action (Buy, Sell, or Hold). api/TradingSimulator.Strategies.TradingD.title: Enum TradingDecisionEnum | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/TradingSimulator.Strategies.TradingD.summary: Enum TradingDecisionEnum Defines the possible trading decisions that can be made by a trading strategy. This enum is used throughout the trading simulator to represent the core actions a strategy can recommend: purchasing an asset, maintaining the current position, or selling an existing position. It serves as the fundamental decision type for strategy evaluation and trade execution logic. Namespace: TradingSimulator.Strategies Assembly: TradingSimulator.dll Syntax public enum TradingDecisionEnum Fields Name Description Buy Indicates a recommendation to purchase or enter a long position in the asset. This decision is typically generated when strategy signals suggest upward price movement or favorable market conditions for buying. Hold Indicates a recommendation to maintain the current position without making changes. This decision is used when strategy signals do not strongly suggest buying or selling, or when the market conditions are neutral or uncertain. Sell Indicates a recommendation to sell or exit a long position in the asset. This decision is typically generated when strategy signals suggest downward price movement or unfavorable market conditions for holding the current position.

api/TradingSimulator.TradingSimulatorSer.summary: Class TradingSimulatorService Core service for orchestrating trading strategy simulations and backtesting operations. This service manages the complete lifecycle of running trading strategies against historical market snapshots, including data loading, strategy execution, performance analysis, and result reporting. It integrates with various components like DataLoader, MarketProcessor, and StrategyResolver to provide comprehensive simulation capabilities for evaluating trading strategies. Features include configurable cache directory and timeout values, input validation with warnings for invalid parameters, and async operations for high-throughput scenarios. api/TradingSimulator.html.title: Namespace TradingSimulator | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/TradingSimulator.html.summary: Namespace TradingSimulator Classes CachedMarketData Contains cached market data for a specific market, including profit/loss, position information, and various lists of price points representing different market events and states. This class serves as the primary data structure for storing processed market simulation results that can be serialized to JSON for persistence and later visualization in the GUI. Enhanced with validation, deep cloning, and serialization controls. DataLoader DiscrepancyDetectionConfig Configuration for discrepancy detection parameters. MarketProcessor Processes individual markets in the trading simulator by running trading strategies, analyzing event logs, and generating visualization data points for market analysis. This class orchestrates the complete market processing pipeline from simulation to data persistence. MarketProcessorConfig Configuration class for MarketProcessor settings. PerformanceMetrics Performance metrics class for tracking message processing rates and queue depths. PricePoint Represents a point in time with a price value and optional memo for market data visualization. This record is used throughout the trading simulator to store timestamped price information with associated metadata for charting and analysis purposes. StrategyResolver Central resolver for trading strategy families in the trading simulator. This class acts as a bridge between strategy family enums and their concrete implementations, providing a unified interface for accessing training mappings and parameter sets for different trading strategies. It encapsulates the logic for resolving strategy families to their corresponding configurations and mappings. TradePoint Represents a trade point with timestamp, price, and trading decision. This record encapsulates the essential data for a trading action in the simulator. TradingContext TradingSimulatorService Core service for orchestrating trading strategy simulations and backtesting operations. This service manages the complete lifecycle of running trading strategies against historical market snapshots, including data loading, strategy execution, performance analysis, and result reporting. It integrates with various components like DataLoader, MarketProcessor, and StrategyResolver to provide comprehensive simulation capabilities for evaluating trading strategies. Features include configurable cache directory and timeout values, input validation with warnings for invalid parameters, and async operations for high-throughput scenarios. Enums TradingSimulatorService.StrategyFamily Delegates TradingStrategyFunc<T>

api/TradingStrategies.ActionDecision.htm.title: Class ActionDecision | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/TradingStrategies.ActionDecision.htm.summary: Class ActionDecision Represents the output of a trading strategy's decision-making process in the Kalshi trading bot system. This class encapsulates the recommended trading action, including the action type, associated parameters for order execution, and explanatory information. It serves as the communication mechanism between trading strategies and the simulation or execution engine, providing all necessary details for processing the recommended trade action. The Type property determines the primary action (Buy, Sell, Exit, None, etc.), while Price and Quantity specify order parameters for limit orders. Expiration allows for time-limited orders, and Memo provides context for the decision that can be used for logging, analysis, or debugging. api/TradingStrategies.Classification.Sna.title: Class SnapshotPeriodHelper | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/TradingStrategies.Classification.Sna.summary: Class SnapshotPeriodHelper Provides functionality for processing and grouping market snapshots into valid analysis periods. This class implements the core logic for identifying continuous market periods based on time gaps and price stability, ensuring that only stable market conditions are used for trading strategy evaluation and backtesting. It handles the serialization of snapshot groups to disk and their subsequent loading for analysis. Supports async operations, parallel processing, and progress reporting. Inheritance object SnapshotPeriodHelper Implements ISnapshotPeriodHelper Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: TradingStrategies.Classification Assembly: TradingStrategies.dll Syntax public class SnapshotPeriodHelper : ISnapshotPeriodHelper Remarks The class uses configurable thresholds for time gaps (SmallGapMinutes and MaxActiveGapHours) and price changes (PriceChangeThreshold) to determine period boundaries. Snapshots are grouped when they are temporally close and price changes are minimal, allowing for meaningful analysis of market behavior during stable periods. Configuration is injected via constructor for flexibility. Async file I/O improves performance with large snapshot groups, parallel processing accelerates data flattening, and progress reporting enables monitoring of long-running operations. Constructors | Edit this page View Source SnapshotPeriodHelper(SnapshotConfig) Initializes a new instance of the SnapshotPeriodHelper class with the specified configuration. Declaration public SnapshotPeriodHelper(SnapshotConfig config) Parameters Type Name Description SnapshotConfig config The snapshot configuration containing thresholds and settings. Methods | Edit this page View Source LoadSnapshotGroup(string) Loads a snapshot group from a JSON file and reconstructs the original snapshot DTOs. This method reverses the flattening process performed during group creation, converting the stored flat data back into structured SnapshotDTO objects with proper JSON formatting. Declaration public Task<List<SnapshotDTO>> LoadSnapshotGroup(string filePath) Parameters Type Name Description string filePath The full path to the JSON file containing the flattened snapshot group data. Returns Type Description Task<List<SnapshotDTO>> A task containing a list of reconstructed SnapshotDTO objects, ordered by snapshot date. Remarks The loading process: Reads the JSON file containing flattened snapshot data Deserializes into a list of flattened dictionaries Reconstructs each snapshot's RawJSON by mapping flattened fields back to MarketSnapshot structure Orders snapshots by timestamp for consistency Enhances snapshots with market type information for downstream processing Error handling ensures that file reading or parsing failures don't crash the application, with errors logged for debugging while returning any successfully processed snapshots. | Edit this page View Source SplitIntoValidGroups(List<SnapshotDTO>, string, IProgress<double>?) Splits a sequence of market snapshots into valid analysis periods based on time continuity and price stability criteria. Snapshots are grouped together when they represent a continuous market period without significant price changes or excessive time gaps. Declaration public Task<List<SnapshotGroupDTO>> SplitIntoValidGroups(List<SnapshotDTO> snapshots, string snapshotDirectory, IProgress<double>? progress = null) Parameters Type Name Description List<SnapshotDTO> snapshots The ordered list of market snapshots to process. Must be sorted by timestamp. string snapshotDirectory The directory path where snapshot group files will be saved. IProgress<double> progress Optional progress reporter for long-running operations. Returns Type Description Task<List<SnapshotGroupDTO>> A task representing the asynchronous operation, containing a list of snapshot groups. Remarks The method processes snapshots sequentially, starting a new group when: Time gaps exceed the maximum active gap threshold, or Price changes exceed the threshold (unless within the small gap tolerance) Each group is saved as a JSON file and returned as a SnapshotGroupDTO containing start/end times, price ranges, liquidity metrics, and file path information. Implements ISnapshotPeriodHelper

api/TradingStrategies.Configuration.Simu.summary: Class SimulationConfig Configuration class containing simulation-related parameters used throughout the trading bot system. Values are injected via dependency injection and used by StrategySimulation class for accurate market simulation. Includes comprehensive data validation to prevent invalid parameter combinations. api/TradingStrategies.Configuration.Snap.summary: Class SnapshotConfig Configuration class containing snapshot-related parameters used throughout the trading bot system. These settings control snapshot timing tolerances, schema versioning, and data persistence behavior for market data snapshots used in trading strategy evaluation and backtesting. api/TradingStrategies.Extensions.MarketS.title: Class MarketSnapshotExtensions | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/TradingStrategies.Extensions.MarketS.summary: Class MarketSnapshotExtensions Provides extension methods for MarketSnapshot to compute and update order book metrics from simulated order book data. This class serves as a bridge between the raw SimulatedOrderbook data structure and the calculated metrics stored in MarketSnapshot, enabling efficient computation of spreads, depths, volumes, and other trading indicators. All calculations are performed in a stateless manner, ensuring thread safety and reusability across different market simulation scenarios. Inheritance object MarketSnapshotExtensions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: TradingStrategies.Extensions Assembly: TradingStrategies.dll Syntax public static class MarketSnapshotExtensions Methods | Edit this page View Source UpdateOrderbookMetricsFromSimulated(MarketSnapshot, SimulatedOrderbook) Updates all order book-related metrics in the MarketSnapshot based on the current state of the SimulatedOrderbook. This method recalculates spreads, depths, volumes, ranges, imbalances, and center of mass values to reflect the latest order book state. It is typically called after order book modifications during trading simulations to ensure all derived metrics remain consistent with the underlying order book data. Includes input validation and performance timing for robustness and monitoring. Declaration public static void UpdateOrderbookMetricsFromSimulated(this MarketSnapshot snapshot, SimulatedOrderbook book) Parameters Type Name Description MarketSnapshot snapshot The MarketSnapshot instance whose metrics will be updated. SimulatedOrderbook book The SimulatedOrderbook containing the current order book state to analyze. Remarks This method performs comprehensive order book analysis including: Best bid/ask price identification Spread calculations between bid and ask prices Depth analysis at best prices and within tolerance ranges Volume calculations in both contract count and dollar value Price range analysis for bid orders Imbalance calculations between yes/no sides Center of mass calculations for order book distribution All calculations use the snapshot's TolerancePercentage for range-based metrics and assume prices are in cents (1-99 range) as per Kalshi market conventions. Includes input validation to prevent null reference exceptions and performance timing measurement for monitoring execution time in high-frequency scenarios.

api/TradingStrategies.Extensions.PseudoC.title: Class PseudoCandlestickExtensions | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/TradingStrategies.Extensions.PseudoC.summary: Class PseudoCandlestickExtensions Provides extension methods for converting PseudoCandlestick collections to standard candlestick formats. This class serves as a bridge between simplified mid-price candlestick data and traditional OHLC candlestick data, enabling pattern detection and technical analysis algorithms to work with market data. Inheritance object PseudoCandlestickExtensions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: TradingStrategies.Extensions Assembly: TradingStrategies.dll Syntax public static class PseudoCandlestickExtensions Methods | Edit this page View Source ToCandleMids(IList<PseudoCandlestick>, string, TradingConfig, ILogger) Converts a sequence of PseudoCandlesticks to an array of CandleMids with OHLC data. Each resulting candlestick represents the price movement between consecutive PseudoCandlesticks, using the previous candlestick's mid-close as the open price and the current candlestick's mid-close as the close price, with high/low from the current candlestick. Declaration public static CandleMids[] ToCandleMids(this IList<PseudoCandlestick> candles, string marketTicker, TradingConfig config = null, ILogger logger = null) Parameters Type Name Description IList<PseudoCandlestick> candles The sequence of PseudoCandlesticks to convert. Must contain at least 2 elements. string marketTicker The market ticker identifier to assign to each resulting CandleMids. TradingConfig config Optional trading configuration for volume precision handling. ILogger logger Optional logger for performance metrics collection. Returns Type Description CandleMids[] An array of CandleMids representing the price movements between consecutive input candlesticks. Remarks This method creates n-1 candlesticks from n input PseudoCandlesticks, where each output candlestick represents the transition from one time period to the next. The volume is cast from decimal to double to match the CandleMids data type expectations, with optional precision rounding based on config. Performance metrics are logged if the conversion takes longer than 100ms for large sequences. Exceptions Type Condition ArgumentNullException Thrown when candles is null. ArgumentException Thrown when candles contains fewer than 2 elements or marketTicker is null/empty.

api/TradingStrategies.Strategies.Signal..title: Enum Signal | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/TradingStrategies.Strategies.Signal..summary: Enum Signal Defines the various trading signals used by the strategy system to identify market conditions and technical indicators. These signals represent different market states, technical analysis patterns, and external factors that can influence trading decisions. Each signal corresponds to a specific condition that can be evaluated against market data to determine appropriate trading actions. Namespace: TradingStrategies.Strategies Assembly: TradingStrategies.dll Syntax public enum Signal Fields Name Description AbsPositionAboveMax Absolute position size is above the maximum allowed threshold. ActiveYes Yes side showing active trading interest. AtrMediumHigh Average True Range (ATR) indicates medium to high volatility. BearishMomentum Bearish momentum indicators are present. BearishVolume Bearish volume patterns detected. BidImbalance Bid side shows imbalance compared to ask side. BreakoutDown Price breaking out to the downside. BreakoutUp Price breaking out to the upside. BullishMomentum Bullish momentum indicators are present. BullishVolume Bullish volume patterns detected. BuyFlow Strong buying flow detected in the market. BuyPressure Strong buying pressure detected. CryptoNews Cryptocurrency-related news impacting markets. DepthAtBestYesAskLow Depth at the best Yes ask is low, indicating thin liquidity on the sell side. DepthAtBestYesBidLow Depth at the best Yes bid is low, indicating thin liquidity on the buy side. ElectionNearing Election timing is nearing, potentially affecting market volatility. ExtremeWeather Extreme weather conditions affecting market sentiment. FadeHigh Price fading from recent highs. FadeLow Price fading from recent lows. HighVolBuy High volume buying activity detected. HighVolSell High volume selling activity detected. LargeBuyTrades Large buy trades executed. LiveEvent Live event occurring that may impact market outcomes. MacdAboveSignal MACD line is above the signal line, indicating bullish momentum. MacdBelowSignal MACD line is below the signal line, indicating bearish momentum. MacroEvent Macroeconomic event influencing market conditions. MidAboveBollingerUpper Mid price is above the upper Bollinger Band, suggesting potential reversal or overextension. MidAboveEmaMedium Mid price is above the medium-term EMA. MidAboveEmaPlusAtr Mid price is above the EMA plus ATR band, indicating potential overbought conditions. MidAboveVwap Mid price is above the Volume Weighted Average Price (VWAP). MidBelowBollingerLower Mid price is below the lower Bollinger Band, suggesting potential reversal or overextension. MidBelowEmaMedium Mid price is below the medium-term EMA. MidBelowEmaMinusAtr Mid price is below the EMA minus ATR band, indicating potential oversold conditions. MidBelowVwap Mid price is below the Volume Weighted Average Price (VWAP). NearResistance Price is near a resistance level, potentially indicating a reversal opportunity. NearSupport Price is near a support level, potentially indicating a bounce opportunity. NegativeHistogram MACD histogram shows negative divergence. NegativeOBV On-Balance Volume (OBV) shows negative divergence. ObvMedium On-Balance Volume (OBV) indicates medium-term accumulation/distribution. Overbought Market indicators suggest overbought conditions. Oversold Market indicators suggest oversold conditions. PositiveHistogram MACD histogram shows positive divergence. PositiveOBV On-Balance Volume (OBV) shows positive divergence. RateCutExpectation Market expectation of interest rate cuts. RsiShort RSI indicates short-term overbought conditions. SeasonalPattern Seasonal pattern influencing market behavior. SellFlow Strong selling flow detected in the market. SellPressure Strong selling pressure detected. StochasticK Stochastic K oscillator reading. TeamFavorite Team or competitor favoritism influencing market sentiment. TradeVolumePerMinuteNoHigh Trade volume per minute for No contracts is high. TradeVolumePerMinuteYesHigh Trade volume per minute for Yes contracts is high. UndervaluedCandidate Asset appears to be undervalued relative to fundamentals. VelocityPerMinuteTopNoBid Velocity per minute at the top No bid price level. VelocityPerMinuteTopYesBid Velocity per minute at the top Yes bid price level. WideSpreadBuy Wide spread conditions favoring buy orders. WideSpreadSell Wide spread conditions favoring sell orders.

api/TradingStrategies.Strategies.SignalC.title: Class SignalCondition | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/TradingStrategies.Strategies.SignalC.summary: Class SignalCondition Defines a condition based on a trading signal that triggers a specific trading action. This class combines a signal (technical indicator or market condition), a comparison operator, a threshold value, and the resulting action type to create rule-based trading logic. Signal conditions are used by strategies to evaluate market states and determine appropriate responses. api/TradingStrategies.Strategies.Strat.h.title: Class Strat | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/TradingStrategies.Strategies.Strat.h.summary: Class Strat Abstract base class for individual trading strategy implementations. Concrete strategy classes inherit from this class to provide specific trading logic that evaluates market conditions and returns trading decisions. Each strategy has a weight that influences its importance when combined with other strategies in a composite approach. api/TradingStrategies.Strategies.Strateg.title: Class Strategy | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/TradingStrategies.Strategies.Strateg.summary: Class Strategy Represents a composite trading strategy that combines multiple individual strategies (Strats). This class aggregates the decisions from multiple strategies based on their weights, implementing a voting system where the strategy with the highest combined weight for a particular action wins. The composite approach allows for more robust trading decisions by considering multiple perspectives. Inheritance object Strategy Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: TradingStrategies.Strategies Assembly: TradingStrategies.dll Syntax public class Strategy Constructors | Edit this page View Source Strategy(string, List<Strat>) Initializes a new instance of the Strategy class with a name and list of individual strategies. Declaration public Strategy(string name, List<Strat> strats) Parameters Type Name Description string name The name identifier for this strategy. List<Strat> strats The list of individual strategies to combine in this composite strategy. Properties | Edit this page View Source Name Gets the name of this strategy for identification and logging purposes. Declaration public string Name { get; } Property Value Type Description string | Edit this page View Source Strats Gets the read-only list of individual strategies that make up this composite strategy. Declaration public IReadOnlyList<Strat> Strats { get; } Property Value Type Description IReadOnlyList<Strat> Methods | Edit this page View Source GetAction(MarketSnapshot, MarketSnapshot?, int) Evaluates all individual strategies and returns the aggregated trading decision. Uses a weighted voting system where each strategy's weight contributes to its action type. The action type with the highest total weight is selected, and among strategies voting for that type, the one with the highest individual weight provides the final decision details. Declaration public ActionDecision GetAction(MarketSnapshot snapshot, MarketSnapshot? previousSnapshot, int simulationPosition = 0) Parameters Type Name Description MarketSnapshot snapshot The current market snapshot containing price, volume, and technical indicator data. MarketSnapshot previousSnapshot The previous market snapshot for comparison, or null if this is the first snapshot. int simulationPosition The current position in the simulation (0 for no position, positive for long, negative for short). Returns Type Description ActionDecision An ActionDecision containing the recommended trading action and associated metadata from the highest-weighted strategy.

api/TradingStrategies.Strategies.html.title: Namespace TradingStrategies.Strategies | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/TradingStrategies.Strategies.html.summary: Namespace TradingStrategies.Strategies Classes Scenario Represents a trading scenario that organizes strategies based on different market conditions. This class serves as a container for mapping market types to their corresponding trading strategies, allowing the system to select appropriate strategies based on current market conditions during simulation or live trading. SignalCondition Defines a condition based on a trading signal that triggers a specific trading action. This class combines a signal (technical indicator or market condition), a comparison operator, a threshold value, and the resulting action type to create rule-based trading logic. Signal conditions are used by strategies to evaluate market states and determine appropriate responses. Strat Abstract base class for individual trading strategy implementations. Concrete strategy classes inherit from this class to provide specific trading logic that evaluates market conditions and returns trading decisions. Each strategy has a weight that influences its importance when combined with other strategies in a composite approach. Strategy Represents a composite trading strategy that combines multiple individual strategies (Strats). This class aggregates the decisions from multiple strategies based on their weights, implementing a voting system where the strategy with the highest combined weight for a particular action wins. The composite approach allows for more robust trading decisions by considering multiple perspectives. Enums Signal Defines the various trading signals used by the strategy system to identify market conditions and technical indicators. These signals represent different market states, technical analysis patterns, and external factors that can influence trading decisions. Each signal corresponds to a specific condition that can be evaluated against market data to determine appropriate trading actions.

api/TradingStrategies.Trading.Helpers.Ma.summary: Class MarketTypeHelper Provides functionality to classify market types based on various trading conditions extracted from market snapshots. This helper uses a rule-based system with priority ordering to determine the most appropriate market type for a given set of market conditions, enabling trading strategies to adapt their behavior accordingly. Inheritance object MarketTypeHelper Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: TradingStrategies.Trading.Helpers Assembly: TradingStrategies.dll Syntax public class MarketTypeHelper Remarks The classification process involves: Extracting condition values (price movement, liquidity, closing time, etc.) from a MarketSnapshot Matching these conditions against predefined rules with associated priorities Returning the highest-priority matching market type This class is instantiated as needed and used in simulation and analysis pipelines to categorize market states for strategy selection and performance evaluation. Constructors | Edit this page View Source MarketTypeHelper() Initializes a new instance of the MarketTypeHelper class. Declaration public MarketTypeHelper() Remarks Sets up the internal mappings collection and populates it with predefined market type rules based on various combinations of market conditions and their associated priorities. Methods | Edit this page View Source AddBroadMapping(MarketType, Conditions_PriceMovement?, Conditions_Liquidity?, Conditions_ClosingTime?, Conditions_ActivityLevel?, Conditions_UncertaintySignal?, Conditions_MarketCategory?, int) Adds a market type mapping using individual condition parameters for convenience. Declaration public void AddBroadMapping(StrategyEnums.MarketType marketType, StrategyEnums.Conditions_PriceMovement? priceMovement = null, StrategyEnums.Conditions_Liquidity? liquidity = null, StrategyEnums.Conditions_ClosingTime? closingTime = null, StrategyEnums.Conditions_ActivityLevel? activityLevel = null, StrategyEnums.Conditions_UncertaintySignal? uncertaintySignal = null, StrategyEnums.Conditions_MarketCategory? marketCategory = null, int priority = 0) Parameters Type Name Description StrategyEnums.MarketType marketType The market type to assign when conditions are met. StrategyEnums.Conditions_PriceMovement? priceMovement Optional price movement condition requirement. StrategyEnums.Conditions_Liquidity? liquidity Optional liquidity condition requirement. StrategyEnums.Conditions_ClosingTime? closingTime Optional closing time condition requirement. StrategyEnums.Conditions_ActivityLevel? activityLevel Optional activity level condition requirement. StrategyEnums.Conditions_UncertaintySignal? uncertaintySignal Optional uncertainty signal condition requirement. StrategyEnums.Conditions_MarketCategory? marketCategory Optional market category condition requirement. int priority Priority level for this mapping (higher values take precedence in conflicts). Remarks This method provides a convenient way to add mappings by specifying conditions as individual parameters rather than as a collection. Only non-null conditions are included in the mapping. This is the primary method used in InitializeMarketTypeMap for setting up the predefined rules. | Edit this page View Source AddPriorityMapping(MarketType, IEnumerable<(string condition, Enum value)>, int) Adds a market type mapping using a collection of condition-value pairs. Declaration public void AddPriorityMapping(StrategyEnums.MarketType marketType, IEnumerable<(string condition, Enum value)> conditions, int priority = 0) Parameters Type Name Description StrategyEnums.MarketType marketType The market type to assign when conditions are met. IEnumerable<(string condition, Enum value)> conditions Collection of condition names and their required enum values. int priority Priority level for this mapping (higher values take precedence in conflicts). Remarks This method allows for more flexible condition specification compared to AddBroadMapping, supporting any combination of conditions as key-value pairs. The conditions are converted to a dictionary for efficient matching during resolution. | Edit this page View Source GetMarketType(MarketSnapshot) Determines the market type for a given market snapshot by extracting conditions and resolving them against mappings. Declaration public StrategyEnums.MarketType GetMarketType(MarketSnapshot snapshot) Parameters Type Name Description MarketSnapshot snapshot The market snapshot containing all relevant trading data and indicators. Returns Type Description StrategyEnums.MarketType The most appropriate market type based on current conditions, or MarketType.Undefined if no match is found. Remarks This is the primary public method for market type classification. It extracts six key condition types from the snapshot (price movement, liquidity, closing time, activity level, uncertainty signal, and market category) and uses them to find the best matching market type rule based on priority and condition specificity. The method is designed to be called during market analysis, simulation, and strategy selection phases.
